cards.question: Page 11 in 'About this Guide' (General section) - detail 1: Explain 'About this Guide Intended audience M3' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: About this Guide Intended audience M3 Business Engine User Documentation provides guidance for end users and consultants regarding understanding basic concepts and using key processes in M3 Business Engine. Further information about the available programs and functionality is available as field help texts. Document structure M3 Business Engine User Documentation is a task-oriented documentation, providing descriptions on performing specific procedures, defining settings, and running specific, step-by-step procedures. To some extent, this documentation set also contains conceptual documents, providing background information or describing requirements and how they are matched in M3 Business Engine.

cards.question: Page 11 in 'About this Guide' (General section) - detail 2: Explain 'This table provides a brief overview' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: This table provides a brief overview of the most common sections that appear in this document. Briefly describes what kind of information the document provides.Introduction Describes the consequence of a process completed or a concept run.Outcome Explains how the results can be used.Uses Describes, if applicable, any changes that have been implemented in M3. How the system is affected Describes the prerequisites of a process or a concept.Before you start Lists all relevant parameters with a detailed explanation.Parameters to set Describes, if applicable, the concept or the purpose of the concept and when and how it is run. Description Provides an overview (often as a flow chart) of the activities in the pro- cess.

cards.question: Page 11 in 'About this Guide' (General section) - detail 3: Explain 'Outline Describes all the activities above' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Outline Describes all the activities above and provides a summary of when, where and how to carry them out. Activity description Describes, if applicable, how to carry out a settings instruction.Follow these steps Lists other topics that contain relevant information.Related topics M3 Application Foundation User Guide | About this Guide

cards.question: Page 12 in 'About this Guide' (Contacting Infor) - detail 3: Explain 'For the latest documentation, go to' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.

cards.question: Page 13 in 'System Administration' (Create and Change a System Calendar) - detail 1: Explain 'Chapter 1: System Administration Create and' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Chapter 1: System Administration Create and Change a System Calendar This document explains how you create a system calendar and define days in the system calendar. Outcome You have created a system calendar for a division (a system calendar must be created for each division). Normally at least two calendar years from the current date should have been defined. For MUC configuration, a system calendar must be defined for each of the divisions included in the configuration. This means that a calendar must be defined for the central (blank) division, even if the division is not used to process information. You have changed one or several values in these fields: • Generated capacity on each day • Production days • Goods receiving days • Delivery days. When a date is specified in M3, the system checks that the date has been defined in the system calendar. The system calendar is stored in the CSYCAL file.

cards.question: Page 13 in 'System Administration' (Create and Change a System Calendar) - detail 2: Explain 'Automatic generation of system calendar The' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Automatic generation of system calendar The system calendar and system periods can be automatically generated, if required, using the fix program F16MMSYS01 which runs after every patch. To generate, the fix program uses the field 'Generated calendar years' in 'Company. Open' (MNS095). The field enables the user to set how many years ahead the system calendar and periods are generated. If the number of years is not specified, meaning if the field is left blank or has the value 0, the default value of two years is used. You can use values between 2-99. However, if automatic generation is not required, specify - in the field. When F16MMSYS01 is run, it checks the date of the current day together with 'Generated calendar years' in (MNS095) to see if there are enough years in the system calendar and periods for each division and company.

cards.question: Page 13 in 'System Administration' (Create and Change a System Calendar) - detail 3: Explain 'When the generation is run, the' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: When the generation is run, the calendar settings in 'System Calendar. Create Days' (CRS905) are used. By using action F14 in (CRS905), you can set your own, customized calendar settings as the standard settings. F16MMSYS01 does not generate system period types that do not exist at the time of the generation – it always looks at the current year to see which types exist and therefore which to generate. For example, in year 2021, the period types 1, 2, and exist. When the generation is started in year 2021, only the types 1, 2, and are M3 Application Foundation User Guide | System Administration

cards.question: Page 14 in 'System Administration' (Create and Change a System Calendar) - detail 1: Explain 'generated for X years ahead. The' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: generated for X years ahead. The types 4, 5, and exist for year only and are therefore not included. This is to avoid generation of system periods that are not used. If the current year does not have any system periods at all at the time of the generation, the periods are still generated. The types to generate in this case are the ones specified in 'Company. Connect Division' (MNS100) for each company and division. Since the patching is done in the middle of the month, F16MMSYS01 must check based on the following month if there are enough years generated. If there are years already generated, the fix program does nothing. If generation is required, the fix program generates the entire years. When the calendar and system periods have been generated, an M3 application message is sent to the system admin person that calendar and system periods were created for division x, year y. If there is an error, and the system calendar or system peri

cards.question: Page 14 in 'System Administration' (Create and Change a System Calendar) - detail 2: Explain 'The fix program then checks if' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: The fix program then checks if May exists years from – May exists, but not May 2024. The entire year of 2024, January-December, is then generated. Next month, in May 2022, the fix program checks if June exists years from – both June and June exist. Therefore, the fix program does nothing more, until next month when it checks again. Before you start • You must be in the correct division and commands to switch between divisions must be defined in 'Function. Open' (MNS110). You must have authority for the divisions. Authority is assigned in 'User. Open' (MNS150). • If you want to change a day in 'System Calendar. Open' (CRS900), a system calendar must have been defined in 'System Calendar. Create Days' (CRS905). Follow these steps to create a system calendar To create the system calendar, start 'System Calendar. Open' (CRS900).

cards.question: Page 14 in 'System Administration' (Create and Change a System Calendar) - detail 3: Explain 'Select 'Settings' (F13) on the B' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Select 'Settings' (F13) on the B panel. On the P panel, select 'Generate calendar' (F13). This displays 'System Calendar. Create Days' (CRS905/A). Specify a range in the fields 'From Year' and 'To year'. Specify values in these fields on the E panel: 'General capacity,' 'Production day,' 'Goods receiving day,' and 'Delivery day.' Select 'Start generation' (F13). The generation takes place online and not in a batch job. Repeat the procedure to define one more calendar year, since you should define at least two calendar years. Press F3. Follow these steps to change a system calendar To define the system calendar day, start 'System Calendar. Open' (CRS900/B). Select a date in the display file, then on the File menu, click Open. Specify values in these fields on the E panel: 'General capacity,' 'Production day,' 'Goods receiving day,' and 'Delivery day.' M3 Application Foundation User Guide | System Administration

cards.question: Page 15 in 'System Administration' (Create and Change a System Calendar) - detail 1: Explain 'Click Next to finish. Follow these' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Click Next to finish. Follow these steps to automatically create a system calendar If you do not want your own, customized calendar settings as the standard settings, proceed to step 5. Start 'System Calendar. Create Days' (CRS905). Specify values in these fields on the E panel: 'General capacity', 'Production day', 'Banking day', 'Adjustment days payment day', 'Adjustment days due date', 'Goods receiving day', and 'Delivery day'. Select 'Set as standard' (F14). A message that states the calendar settings have been set as standard is displayed. Press F3 to close the program. Start 'Company.

cards.question: Page 15 in 'System Administration' (Create and Change a System Calendar) - detail 2: Explain 'Open' (MNS095). Select the company that' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Open' (MNS095). Select the company that should have automatic generation of system calendar. On the E panel, specify the number of years ahead the calendar should be generated in field 'Generated calendar years'. Specify - if automatic generation is not preferred. Click Next and repeat steps and for all relevant companies. When done, close the program by pressing F3. The fix program checks if generation is required after each patch. Parameters to set The field indicates …FieldProgram ID/Pan- el … the portion of a workday that can be load planned. This portion is entered as a percentage for each day.

cards.question: Page 15 in 'System Administration' (Create and Change a System Calendar) - detail 3: Explain 'Example A: A company normally works' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Example A: A company normally works hours on Mondays. If on a certain Monday it only works hours, 50% should be entered as the general capacity percentage in (CRS900) for that Monday. If the employees in a certain work center only work hours this particular Monday, 50% should be entered as the actual capacity percentage for the current combination and for Monday. General capacity(CRS900/E) (CRS905/E) … whether the date selected is flagged as a production day in the system calendar. You cannot create capacity for a work center on days that are not flagged as production days. Production day(CRS900/E) (CRS905/E) … whether the actual day is a so-called bank day in the calendar. The purchase ledger, for instance, uses the ledger in the financial management system. Bank day(CRS905/A) M3 Application Foundation User Guide | System Administration

cards.question: Page 16 in 'System Administration' (Database Update Manager) - detail 1: Explain 'The field indicates …FieldProgram ID/Pan- el' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: The field indicates …FieldProgram ID/Pan- el … the payment day that is displayed as the default according to the day of the week on which the due date falls. Example If the real due date happens to be on a Sunday, you can as default set the payment date to be on the following Monday. During automatic creation of a system period, the adjustment days are updated as follows: • Mon payment default Tuesday • Tue payment default same day • Wed payment default same day • Thu payment default same day • Fri payment default same day • Sat payment default Friday • Sun payment default Tuesday Adjustment days payment day (CRS905/A) whether goods can be entered as received in M3 on a specific weekday. Example: If goods receiving can take place on a Monday, enter in the Monday field.

cards.question: Page 16 in 'System Administration' (Database Update Manager) - detail 2: Explain 'If goods receiving cannot take place' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: If goods receiving cannot take place on a Sat- urday, enter in the Saturday field. Goods receiving day (CRS900/E) (CRS905/E) … whether deliveries can be entered in M3 on a specific weekday.Delivery day(CRS900/E) (CRS905/E) From year To year (CRS905/E) Database Update Manager Abstract M3 delivers a new standard for fix programs that enables database updates with full tracabillity and the capability to monitor the run’s progress. Background Many database update programs are delivered for each base and feature pack of M3. A net change report for a base delivery of M3 contains the instructions on how to run these programs.

cards.question: Page 16 in 'System Administration' (Database Update Manager) - detail 3: Explain 'For a new base delivery, the' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: For a new base delivery, the Migration Center will normally manage the migration using a set of programs that makes changes to the database as smoothly as possible. For example, some programs are replaced by SQL scripts in order to improve performance. M3 Application Foundation User Guide | System Administration

cards.question: Page 17 in 'System Administration' (Database Update Manager) - detail 1: Explain 'Limitations The new standard for fix' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Limitations The new standard for fix programs is available for all programs, however, customers who are upgrading from versions prior to 15.x must run older fix programs separately. Before you start Open CMS950. To generate the desired fix program, the version that M3 was upgraded from should be indicated. Set the From Version in the settings panel to indicate the versions for which the fix programs metadata should be generated. Click F16 from the Action menu to generate the metadata. You can also do this by clicking Next and then closing and opening the program. All the fix programs released after the From Version entered in the settings are generated.

cards.question: Page 17 in 'System Administration' (Database Update Manager) - detail 2: Explain 'For more information about running fix' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: For more information about running fix programs, see the M3 Core Installation Guide.. Follow these steps To run a fix program Right click on the fix program and select option (Run). Only programs with status (Ready) can be run. The status may be changed to (Ready) in the detailed panel if needed. To pause a fix program Before you run a program, set the logging increment to indicate when to pause the program. So, if the logging increment is set to then for every records read the program checks for a pause signal to pause. While the program is running, right click on it and select option (Pause migration).

cards.question: Page 17 in 'System Administration' (Database Update Manager) - detail 3: Explain 'To resume a program Right click' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: To resume a program Right click on the paused program in status (Pause) and select option (Run) to resume a fix program. To discard a program Click on the program and select option (Discard). The discarded programs are the programs that are already run in the system using a method other than the database update manger. To delete a program You can only delete programs that are in status (Not found). To delete a program, use the Delete option on any program in status 00. To update a program You can update only certain information directly from the database update manager. To update the sequence number or migration status (with limitations) or migration log increment or migration reference, select option (Change) to change and update a program M3 Application Foundation User Guide | System Administration

cards.question: Page 18 in 'System Administration' (Database Update Manager) - detail 1: Explain 'Call fix program after initial checking' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Call fix program after initial checking Fix Program Fzzzxxmmyy (e.g. F13300MM01) Status, configuration, valid program Check if program should run Status, related program Update as started Status, nbr of records, JNA, JNU, Update as in progress Status Logging (optional) Nbr of read/wrt/upd/dlt Pause (if enabled, optional) Status, Last read key Update as finished Status, nbr of read/wrt/upd/dlt, CSYMIG Migration Programs Outcome After you run a fix program, the data in a related table is adjusted with the required modifications. The status of the fix program in the migration table CSYMIG is updated to (Finished). The number of records in the modified table and the number of read/updated/written/deleted records is written to the migration table. Furthermore, the information about the submitted job is updated, such as job number, job name, user, and start and finish time and dates. If the fix program is safe to run several times, you can re

cards.question: Page 18 in 'System Administration' (Database Update Manager) - detail 2: Explain 'MCE Packages The MCE packages related' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: MCE Packages The MCE packages related to the new fix programs released for a correction may contain the fix program itself and an updated version of migration utility cCMMigrationMetadata. The updated version of the utility contains an array element of newly added fix programs. This means that after you install the MCE package and correctly configure the database update manager, the new fix program automatically appears in the list in status (Ready). Logging increment The logging increment is a feature that lets you track the progress of tables that have a high importance. This feature adds the functionality to log the number of records read and the record updated/written/deleted in runtime. For example, if the logging increment is set to then for every records read the program will log this information. Note: Use caution when you set the logging increment, since it can affect program performance.

cards.question: Page 18 in 'System Administration' (Database Update Manager) - detail 3: Explain 'The recommended logging level is the' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: The recommended logging level is the highest possible. With every increment, the program calls a utility and updates the migration table. In addition, logging is used for the pause option. If the program has the pause option set and the logging increment is set to a value greater than zero, then for every increment the fix program listens for a pause signal to pause its operation. M3 Application Foundation User Guide | System Administration

cards.question: Page 19 in 'System Administration' (Define Number Series) - detail 1: Explain 'CMS950MI - API for database update' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: CMS950MI - API for database update manager The database update manager comes with an API that has the same full functionality as CMS950 itself. Define Number Series Number series are used for assigning unique identities to each individual order or transaction in M3. This document explains how to define number series for customer, distribution and requisition orders, as well as for the M3 financial system, or for customer invoices and internal invoices. Outcome Number series are defined for the one or several of the areas described above. Number series are used for assigning unique IDs (numbers) to each individual order and/or transaction in M3. Each number series is connected to a specific number series type. The number series type indicates the purpose of the number series, for example, customer orders, purchase orders, customer returns, package number, picking list, reclassification number, etc. Number series are stored in the (CSYNBR

cards.question: Page 19 in 'System Administration' (Define Number Series) - detail 2: Explain '• You must be in the' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • You must be in the correct division and commands to switch between divisions must be defined in 'Function. Open' (MNS110). You must have authority for the divisions. Authority is assigned in 'User. Open' (MNS150). Follow these steps Select one of the alternatives and continue with the steps for the selected procedure. Define number series for customer, distribution and requisition order or for the financial system Start 'Number Series. Open' (CRS165/B). Specify the number series type and the identity of the number series.

cards.question: Page 19 in 'System Administration' (Define Number Series) - detail 3: Explain 'Select New. On the E panel' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Select New. On the E panel E, specify a name, describing the purpose of the number series. Specify also the start value and the end value for the number series. If necessary, specify a date when this number series should end, and the identity of the number series that should replace it. Press Enter. The 'Last number' field specifies the last number used in the number series, and is incremented automatically when a number is used. Define number series for customer invoices and internal invoices Start 'Internal Invoice Series. Open' (MFS165/B). M3 Application Foundation User Guide | System Administration

cards.question: Page 20 in 'System Administration' (Define Basic Data for Feature List) - detail 1: Explain 'Specify the identity of the number' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Specify the identity of the number series and the year for which it applies. Select New. On the E panel, specify a name, describing the purpose of the number series. Specify also the start value and the end value of the number series. Press Enter. The 'Last number' field specifies the last number used in the number series, and is incremented automatically when a number is used. Parameters to set The field indicates …FieldProgram ID/Panel …the purpose of each number series.

cards.question: Page 20 in 'System Administration' (Define Basic Data for Feature List) - detail 2: Explain 'Click F1 in this field to' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Click F1 in this field to see the purpose and area (application) for each number series type. Next to each type, information is displayed about which number series can be used for the type. If an asterisk (*) is displayed instead of a number series identity, that means that the number series is optional, which also means that you can define more than one number series for that type. Number series type (CRS165/B) or (MFS165) …the number series.Number series(CRS165/B) or (MFS165) Define Basic Data for Feature List This document explains how you define basic data for a feature list. Basic data includes feature status and control table for default feature status and feature responsible. Define feature status The feature status is used for the review and validation of a new feature. The status can be used together with the 'Responsible' field to build customer defined workflows.

cards.question: Page 20 in 'System Administration' (Define Basic Data for Feature List) - detail 3: Explain 'The field automatically updates the field' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: The field automatically updates the field 'Review progress'. Status 10- indicate 'Under review' and 50- indicate 'Review completed'. Workflow In 'Feature Status. Open' (CMS978), the different status used in the feature review progress are defined. Status 10, 50, and are system-defined and cannot be deleted. Define default status for a new feature When a new feature is installed, a default status is defined depending on a selection table. These fields are used as control objects: • Product • Feature type • Module M3 Application Foundation User Guide | System Administration

cards.question: Page 21 in 'System Administration' (Define Basic Data for Feature List) - detail 1: Explain '• Country • Configuration component Examples' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • Country • Configuration component Examples of how the selection table can be used to set the default status: • I do not want to review feature type (Zero impact), always set to status 90. • I do not use module Fixed Assets, always set to status 90. • I do not use country Sweden, always set to status 90. If no record is found in the selection table that matches the feature, a status is set according to these rules: • Features with type 1='Zero impact' and 2='Functional setting' are set to status '50' by default. • Features with type 3-’Feature toggle, non-disruptive’, 4-‘Feature toggle, disruptive’, 5='Immediately', and 6=’Immediately, any release’ are set to status '10' by default. Workflow Connect control object Start 'Available Object Ctrl Parameters. Open' (CMS016). cards.goodAnswer: Open' (CMS017) is started. If feature selection is missing, generate all standard parameters by using function key F14='Standard'. On (CMS017/B), use option 1='Create'. On the E panel, specify: a Feature select with 1-'Feature status'. b Field 1, 2, and with selections made in field group CMKV1. Press Enter. 'Feature Selection Table. Open' (CMS973) is started. cards.question: Page 21 in 'System Administration' (Define Basic Data for Feature List) - detail 3: Explain 'Open' (CMS973). In Feature select, select' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Open' (CMS973). In Feature select, select 1-'Feature status'. Select the priority. Specify values for the control fields and use option 1='Create'. On the E panel, specify the feature status. Press Enter. Define default responsible person for the new feature When a new feature is installed, a default responsible is defined based on a selection table. These fields are used as control objects: • Product • Feature type • Module • Country • Configuration component Workflow Connect control object M3 Application Foundation User Guide | System Administration

cards.question: Page 22 in 'System Administration' (Define Basic Data for Feature List) - detail 1: Explain 'Start 'Available Object Ctrl Parameters. Open'' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Start 'Available Object Ctrl Parameters. Open' (CMS016). In the Object control parameter column, right-click Feature selection and select option 11='Object table detailed lines'. 'Generic Object Control Table. Open' (CMS017) is started. If feature selection is missing, generate all standard parameters by using function key F14='Standard'. On (CMS017/B), use option 1='Create'. On the E panel, specify: a Feature select with 2-'Responsible'. b Field 1, 2, and with selections made in field group CMKV1. Press Enter. 'Feature Selection Table. Open' (CMS973) is started. Define default status Start 'Feature Selection Table.

cards.question: Page 22 in 'System Administration' (Define Basic Data for Feature List) - detail 2: Explain 'Open' (CMS973). In Feature select, select' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Open' (CMS973). In Feature select, select 2-'Responsible'. Select the priority. Specify values for the control fields and use option 1='Create'. On the E panel, specify the responsible person. Press Enter. Define default activation for the new feature When a new feature of feature type 3-‘Feature toggle’ and 4-‘Feature toggle’ is installed, it can automatically be activated based on a selection table. If no record is found in the selection table that match, the feature is turned off by default. These fields are used as control objects for the selection table: • Product • Feature type • Module • Country • Configuration component Workflow Connect control object Start 'Available Object Ctrl Parameters. Open' (CMS016). In the Object control parameter column, right-click Feature selection and select option 11='Object table detailed lines'. 'Generic Object Control Table. Open' (CMS017) is started.

cards.question: Page 22 in 'System Administration' (Define Basic Data for Feature List) - detail 3: Explain 'If feature selection is missing, generate' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.

cards.question: Page 23 in 'System Administration' (Enter System Periods and Seasonal Curves) - detail 1: Explain 'Specify values for the control fields' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Specify values for the control fields and use option 1='Create'. On the E panel, specify the responsible person. Define application message When a new feature is installed, an application message 013-'New Feature installed' can be created containing the feature ID, feature type, and feature description. Workflow In 'System Settings. Open' (MNS090), define field Company for application messages. The field indicates in which Company the application message is created (needed because a feature does not have any natural relation to a Company). In 'Settings – Application messages' (CRS424) activate Application message or select the check box for 'Activity code' on the E panel. The application message will be created using the default responsible for a feature. If no default responsible is defined, no application message is created.

cards.question: Page 23 in 'System Administration' (Enter System Periods and Seasonal Curves) - detail 2: Explain 'Enter System Periods and Seasonal Curves' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Enter System Periods and Seasonal Curves This document explains how you define system periods and seasonal curves in a MUC configuration. Outcome Up to five systems periods are entered for each specified division and for the blank division. At least one seasonal curve is entered for each specified division and for the blank division. System periods are used to enter different kinds of data that use different periods. For instance, the manufacturing area may have different periods than the financial area. Seasonal curves is used to describe how a value (for example, sales) varies in percent over a year Seasonal curves primarily influence budgeting and forecasting. System periods are stored in the (CSYPER) file, and seasonal curves are stored in the (CSYSCH) file. Before you start • You must know what period interval your company uses for different applications. Periods and curves must be defined for each of the divisions included in a MU

cards.question: Page 23 in 'System Administration' (Enter System Periods and Seasonal Curves) - detail 3: Explain '• You must complete the steps' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • You must complete the steps described in . • You must be in the correct division and commands to switch between divisions must be defined in 'Function. Open' (MNS110). You must have authority for the divisions. Authority is assigned in 'User. Open' (MNS150). M3 Application Foundation User Guide | System Administration

cards.question: Page 24 in 'System Administration' (Enter System Periods and Seasonal Curves) - detail 1: Explain 'Follow these steps Define System Periods' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Follow these steps Define System Periods Start 'Company. Connect Division' (MNS100/B). This instruction is based on panel sequence EF. Select Open for the division and press Enter until you reach the F panel. On the F panel, specify the period type to use for the respective area (MPM, SLS, FIN, etc.) and the number of periods for each area's period type. Press Enter and F3. Start 'System Calendar. Open Period' (CRS910/B). This instruction is based on panel sequence E1. Specify the period type and year.

cards.question: Page 24 in 'System Administration' (Enter System Periods and Seasonal Curves) - detail 2: Explain 'Select New. On the E panel' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Select New. On the E panel, specify the name of each period in the Text field (for example January, February, etc.) and adjust the proposed start date for the period in the 'From date' field, when necessary. Press Enter. In addition to the from date, information is also displayed about the end date, the number of calendar days, workdays, and bank days for each period. Define Seasonal Curve Start 'Seasonal Curve. Open' (CRS915/B). The instruction is based on panel sequence E. Specify period type, year, and an identity for the curve you will create. Select New. On the E panel, specify a description and a name for the curve.

cards.question: Page 24 in 'System Administration' (Enter System Periods and Seasonal Curves) - detail 3: Explain 'Specify a seasonal index for all' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Specify a seasonal index for all the relevant periods in the curve. Press Enter and F3. The total percentage field displays the total of the specified percentages. cards.question: Page 26 in 'System Administration' (Export Data from System Table CSYTAB) - detail 1: Explain 'value SMCD is used for the' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: value SMCD is used for the salesperson program in 'Salesperson. Open' (CRS100). The constant value is not visible in M3, so it is difficult to know which programs use constant values. CSYTAB also have fields for division or language code. Depending on the table, these two fields are either: • Not used, or always blank. Constant value SMCD, used by (CRS100) uses neither division nor language. • Mandatory, or never blank. Constant value FATP, used by 'FA Type. Open' (FAS075) always requires a division. • Optional, which means can either be blank or contain a value. Constant value TEDL, used by 'Delivery Term. Open' (CRS065) can both have a blank or a specified language code. CSYTAB also stores information in the 'Parameter' field, where several different data fields are stored together as one text string.

cards.question: Page 26 in 'System Administration' (Export Data from System Table CSYTAB) - detail 2: Explain 'As some of the programs using' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: As some of the programs using CSYTAB can have different variations in different countries, the 'Parameter' field can contain different kinds of data for the same constant value. Because of these characteristics, exporting and importing data from CSYTAB can be complex. Program 'CSYTAB. Export' (CMS240) has been created to be able to export data from CSYTAB in a simpler manner. Limitation Not all constant values found in CSYTAB have been enabled to be exported through (CMS240). Use alternative 20='Display XML file' to download the XML file as a compressed zip file. If the 'Auth indivd pgm' parameter in 'System Settings. Open' (MNS090) is set to 1-'Individual browse program', you must authorized the user to the program where the data for the constant value is defined to access and export the information. cards.question: Page 27 in 'System Administration' (Manage features) - detail 1: Explain 'Manage features A feature is used' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Manage features A feature is used to describe functionality that fulfills a requirement of an M3 user. New and enhanced features are listed in 'Feature List. Open' (CMS975). New and enhanced features are classified with different types, depending on their impact on M3 users. The release report describes enhancements in M3 on every release. Only these M3 products are actively utilizing (CMS975) to deliver features: • M3 Business Engine • Infor Sales Hub • M3 BOD Processor Feature types Features are divided into six different types: • 1-'Zero impact' Zero impact means that the feature enhancement does not require any training, documentation changes or configuration. An enhancement with 1-'Zero impact' does not impact end users, so it does not require any training or documentation changes. This type of change has no controlling setting and is included in any update on both Feature and Service releases.

cards.question: Page 27 in 'System Administration' (Manage features) - detail 2: Explain 'These are the examples of the' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: These are the examples of the 1-'Zero impact' type: • New fields in API transactions • New API transactions • New bookmark • Changes to default standard views and field groups • 2-'Functional setting' An enhancement with Feature Type 2-'Functional setting' describes either a net-new feature or a feature controlled by functional settings or parameters. These settings are managed only by administrators or a controlled set of users. The setting or parameter is always released with the default value set to Off, and the setting option remains without any expiration date. This type of change is not included in a service release. If the introduction of the setting is controlled by a switch, the enhancement has Feature Type 3-'Feature toggle'. These are the examples of 2-'Functional setting' type: • Features controlled by a new or modified setting, for example, in 'Company. Connect Division' (MNS100) or 'Facility. Open' (CRS008).

cards.question: Page 27 in 'System Administration' (Manage features) - detail 3: Explain '• Features controlled by a new' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • Features controlled by a new or modified setting on order type, FAM function etc. Commonly used main data programs are not regarded as controlling programs. • 3-'Feature toggle' 3-'Feature toggle' type indicates a feature controlled by a feature toggle during a minimum of six months of transition period. A feature toggle is always released with the default value set to Off. There is an optional activation before the end of the transition period. When the toggle expires, the value set to On. This type of change is only included in a Feature Release. • 4-'Feature toggle, process change' M3 Application Foundation User Guide | System Administration

cards.question: Page 28 in 'System Administration' (Manage features) - detail 1: Explain 'Features causing a required process change' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Features causing a required process change has the type 4-'Feature toggle, process change'. If the feature has no process change, the type remains at 3-'Feature toggle'. • 5-'Immediate, feature version' Features causing required process changes that are not controlled by a feature toggle has the type 5-'Immediate, feature version'. This type of change is released without a controlling setting, but is only included in a feature version. Information about the change is published in advance through the early announcement changes, that requires advance customer action. These are the examples of 5-'Immediate, feature version' type: • Changes in appearance of UX that you cannot toggle • Adding length to identifier (unique) fields • Change of existing structure of BOD • 6-'Immediate, any version' Features causing required processes to change, which are released immediately, are categorized as 6-'Immediate, any version'. This type of change is

However, during the transition period, you can also deactivate the feature. The feature toggle is managed in (CMS975). The 'Update schedule' field indicates in what system update the feature is forced to be activated. Until this update the feature can be activated and deactivated as desired.

cards.question: Page 29 in 'System Administration' (Initial Load) - detail 2: Explain 'The field is displayed on the' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.

The field is displayed on the E panel in (CMS975). Manage feature review In (CMS975), the 'Review progress' field indicates the two major steps in the review progress: • 1-'Under review' • 2-'Review completed' The field is automatically updated based on the 'Feature status' field where status 10- indicate 'Under review' and 50- indicate 'Review completed'. Using the feature status, you can define several minor review steps within the two

cards.goodAnswer: The feature statuses are defined per customer, but status 10-'New', 50-'Confirmed', and 90-'Completed' are system-defined and are not deleted. The feature statuses combined with the'Responsible' field enables you to build customer-defined workflows. All feature review fields are managed in (CMS975). Initial Load The initial load functionality is used to send the first instance of an M3 BE dataset to an external application using BODs that are consumed and processed in Infor ION. This document describes how to set up and execute this initial load. Limitations • A maximum of selection fields from the primary table can be included in the initial load data. M3 Application Foundation User Guide | System Administration

cards.question: Page 30 in 'System Administration' (Initial Load) - detail 1: Explain '• It is not possible to' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • It is not possible to use a key field as a selection field. • Data from related tables is not included in the initial load data. These are only used to refine the selection criteria defined for the primary table. Set up the initial load Open 'Initial Load. Open' (EVS006). Specify this information: a Primary table from which data should be sent. b Required fields from the primary table, up to a maximum of fields. c BOD noun. d BOD variant. e BOD sequence number. Optionally specify if related tables should be used in the selection, up to a maximum of related tables.

cards.question: Page 30 in 'System Administration' (Initial Load) - detail 2: Explain 'a If related tables are included' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: a If related tables are included, specify the required fields. Note that data from the related tables is not included in the initial load dataset. Related tables are only used to refine the selection criteria defined for the primary table. Execute the initial load To execute the initial load immediately after setting up in (EVS006), select related option 11='Run' to open 'Initial load Job. Open' (EVS007). The tables and fields for the BOD noun specified in (EVS006) are then listed. Specify whether the BOD verb is 'Show' or 'Sync'. If 'Show' is specified, a logical ID must be specified to identify the receiver of the BOD. Specify the filter criteria for the pre-selected tables and fields using these options: • Blank value = only a record containing a blank value for the field will be included. • Specific values = a specific value or a comma separated list can be added. Records containing any of the specified values for the field will be

cards.goodAnswer: • Range = any records within the range for the field will be included. • Ignored = the field is not included. Click submit to execute the load. Execute using an existing BOD Open (EVS006). Select the required BOD noun from the list displayed and choose related option 11='Run' to open (EVS007). Verify that the selection criteria listed satisfies requirements. Click Submit to execute the load. View initial load history Status and run details for initial loads already executed can be viewed by selecting related option 12='History' in (EVS006). Information such as number of records processed, start time, end time and selection criteria used is displayed. M3 Application Foundation User Guide | System Administration

cards.question: Page 43 in 'System Administration' (M3 Data Import) - detail 1: Explain 'M3 Data Import M3 Data Import' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: M3 Data Import M3 Data Import allows you to import data located in stream files into the M3 database through APIs and function programs. The use of APIs and function programs ensures that the M3 business logic is utilized for validations, that data is inserted in the proper locations, and that it is done with the proper authorization controls. The source files are copied into the M3 File Transfer area where one of the two programs is executed to read the files, format the proper API and function program transactions, and call the relevant API or function program. The programs are executed in the background, thereby giving the system a better control over the execution. Limitations Supported input files: • .csv (Comma Separated Values) text files having UTF- encoding. • Microsoft Excel files in XML format having the .xlsx extension. To ensure that error logs are generated properly, the recommended file name should not be more than charac

cards.question: Page 43 in 'System Administration' (M3 Data Import) - detail 2: Explain 'It is the naming that tells' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: It is the naming that tells the import program what API and transaction should be used. The format for an API is: 'FreeName-MIProgram.Transaction.csv'. For example: 'NewCustomers-CRS610MI.Add.csv'. The format for a function program is: “FreeName-FncProgram.pList.MaintainMode.csv”. For example: 'Test-MMS015Fnc.INmaintain.ADD.csv'. • Data format The .csv files must contain the names of the transaction input fields as a header, the first line in the file. Only the fields that you require need to be available, for example, key fields and the data to import. The header, as well as the whole file content parts must be separated by either a comma (,) or a semicolon (;).

Thousand separators are not supported. Dates must be specified as 'yyyymmdd'. Numeric decimal separator should always be a decimal point (.). This table shows a simple example of the data for a .csv file for an API. cards.question: Page 46 in 'System Administration' (M3 Data Import) - detail 1: Explain 'Execution The source files must be' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Execution The source files must be placed on the M3 File Transfer Disk, in the FileImport directory. To do this, use the M3 Business Engine Files app in the Administration Tools menu. When it is time to run the import, this is done in 'M3 Data Import. Open' (EVS100). The program lists all available files in the FileImport directory. Follow these steps: Select the relevant file. Select related option 20='Import'. The correct program is selected based upon the file extension. Programs that run for specific file extensions: • .csv File - EVS101 (Importing .csv format files) • Excel File - EVS102 (Importing Excel format files). You can view the import logs history for each data import file in 'M3 Data Import Log. Open' (EVS110) or you can get redirected from (EVS100) using related option 21='View Import Logs'. A log is created for each data import transaction or per worksheet.

cards.question: Page 46 in 'System Administration' (M3 Data Import) - detail 2: Explain 'It includes details such as the' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: It includes details such as the API program and its completed transaction, start and end of execution in date and time, processed lines (successful and failed), any failed actual lines, and error log file for failed imports. You can also check data import runs in other ways. EVS101 and EVS102 are assigned to the job queue using 'Job. Connect Job Queue' (MNS310). You can monitor the jobs in 'Job. Display History' (MNS320) after executing (EVS100). Message Type messages are seen in 'Application Message. Open' (CRS420) that shows information related to the execution. The message shows the number of records that were read, processed, and failed. Error handling When import is executed, two kinds of error can occur: • Severe errors that canceled the execution • Errors that are returned from the API business logic. When severe errors occur, a pulse message is sent to the user that initiated the import. The message is sent to the Infor Inbox an

cards.question: Page 46 in 'System Administration' (M3 Data Import) - detail 3: Explain 'API errors can be different depending' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: API errors can be different depending on the mistakes found in the input data. These errors are recorded in a result file, together with the input data string. Successful transactions are not included in the result file. The result file is immediately executable after having corrected the data for the failing transactions. When the import is finished, an execution report is sent together with a pulse message. Note: The ProcessPulseAlert BOD must be activated in the BOD Processor. Furthermore, message type '012' must be activated in 'Settings – Application Messages' (CRS424). Concept logging is available for problem investigation. The import programs act on the concept 'mvx.import.debug' that can be turned on in the job log. This concept logging can also be used to filter out only these lines for better readability. M3 Application Foundation User Guide | System Administration

cards.question: Page 47 in 'Job Management' (Auto Start Jobs and Subsystems) - detail 1: Explain 'Chapter 2: Job Management Auto Start' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Chapter 2: Job Management Auto Start Jobs and Subsystems The subsystem management programs are used for auto start jobs only. An auto start job is a batch job doing repetitive work or one-time initialization work. Defined auto start jobs are automatically started each time the ASJ subsystem is started. M3 Business Engine contains a number of predefined auto start jobs. It is possible to run parallel auto start jobs. Authorities for subsystems It is recommended to let only few users have access to the programs that are used to maintain the subsystems. This means that these programs should be provided with restricted access: • 'Subsystem. Open' (MNS050) • 'Subsystem Job. Open' (MNS051) Auto start job subsystems The subsystems used for auto start jobs are managed in one program, 'Subsystem.

Working with subsystem jobs (MNS051/B) displays all the jobs in the subsystem and enables you to add new jobs. You can select F14='Crt standard' to automatically create the default set of jobs, or you can select the related option 22-'Add new instance' to create a new instance of the job manually. You can set the job status depending on your preferred job usage. The Status of a job has these valid values: • 10='Preliminary': This status is for newly created jobs. Jobs with status does not run when the subsystem is started. • 20='Definite': This status is to be set for jobs that should be active. Jobs with status runs when the subsystem is started. • 90='Blocked/expired': This status is for jobs that are not in use. Jobs should not be deleted in the subsystem if they are not

cards.goodAnswer: Some jobs are automatically in status 20, while others are in status upon recreation. Option 11='Sel records' displays 'Subsystem Job. Select Records' (MNS052), where you can select specific jobs to be included. F14='Crt standard' creates the standard auto start jobs. This function creates all auto start job for one subsystem. F19='Start all' starts all jobs in (MNS051). Only jobs that have units of work to process are started. F20='End all' ends all jobs in (MNS051). Option 20='Start Job' starts the selected job. Option 21='End job' ends the selected job. Option 22='Add New Instance' creates a new instance of the selected job. The 'Delay' field shows the time, in seconds, that elapses from when the job has stopped processing units of work until it should start again.

cards.question: Page 48 in 'Job Management' (Auto Start Jobs and Subsystems) - detail 3: Explain 'The processing stops when there are' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: The processing stops when there are no more units of work to process. The delay time entered in 'Subsystem Job. Open' (MNS051) does not affect the batch job manager, CMNGJOB. That time is set in the M3 properties table. When the auto start job is activated by a work table, the delay starts to look for new data when the queue does not contain any more records, that is, when the 'No rec' field is equal to 0. When the auto start job is activated by a transaction table, the delay starts to look for new data after all transactions are run and updated. The 'File' field defines the work table that activates the auto start job. If this record is blank, the auto start job is activated by a transaction table. The 'No records' field shows the number of records that are queued and waiting to be processed by the auto start job. M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Job Management

cards.question: Page 49 in 'Job Management' (Auto Start Jobs and Subsystems) - detail 1: Explain 'The 'Active' field shows if the' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: The 'Active' field shows if the job is active and in use. Only jobs with status can be active. However, jobs with status can also be inactive for certain reasons. The 'Select/Omit' (S/O) field shows if a record is selected with option 11='Sel records' to be displayed in 'Subsystem Job. Select Records' (MNS052), where you can restrict the jobs to be included. For some auto start jobs, a subset of units of work can be selected by using 'Subsystem Job. Select Records' (MNS052). This is useful when more than one instance of a job is needed to increase throughput. For example, one job processes units of work for company and another job processes units of work for company 999. The 'Active change rate' field displays how active the job is during a period of time. It indicates how many status changes occur when the code is executed, for example, READ, READ_LOCK, or CHAIN etc. (same statuses that can be seen in a profiler log). All status change

On (MNS051/E), these fields are displayed: • Program - The program that controls the job. • Delay - See above • File - See above • Company, Division – The company and division for which the job is run. If company is not specified here, the default company and division for the user specified in the 'User' field will be used. If no user is specified in the 'User' field, the default company and division for the user entered in 'Subsystem. Open' (MNS050) will be used. • System Language - The system language for which the job is run. If system language is not specified here, the default system language for the user entered in the 'User' field will be used. (If the user is not connected to a system language, the job will be run with the language of the program.) • User – The user for which the job is run. Selecting records for auto start jobs For some of the auto start jobs, a subset of units of work can be selected via 'Subsystem Job. Select Records' (MNS052). This is useful when more than one instance of a job is needed to increase throughput. For example, one job processes units of work for company and another job processes units of work for company 999. The selection is performed in 'Subsystem Job. Select Records' (MNS052). On panel B, enter the conditions for the records to be selected. The 'Select/Omit' (S/O) field indicates if a record is to be selected. 'Select' = Select the records that have field values within (or equal to) the following values. 'Omit' = Omit the records that have field values within (or equal to) the following values. The 'Selection 1' field shows the lowest and highest value in the current field in order f

cards.question: Page 50 in 'Job Management' (Auto Start Jobs and Subsystems) - detail 1: Explain 'The 'Selection 2' field shows the' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: The 'Selection 2' field shows the lowest and highest value in the current field in order for the record to be selected or omitted for the job. The field can for example contain the value Warehouse. These rules apply to the conditions specified in this panel: If nothing is entered in the S/O field, S=Select is used as default value. If at least one of the entries in the S/O field is O=Omit, everything else is selected. If at least one of the entries in the S/O field is S=Select, everything else is omitted. The specified conditions are evaluated from the top and downwards. When a condition is met, no more conditions are evaluated.

cards.question: Page 50 in 'Job Management' (Auto Start Jobs and Subsystems) - detail 3: Explain '(The second condition is not evaluated.)' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: (The second condition is not evaluated.) Auto start jobs The M3 auto start job subsystem has a number of predefined jobs that are performed according to the settings in the subsystem maintenance programs described in this document. The predefined jobs can be used for purposes such as: • Allocating order lines • Rescheduling manufacturing orders • Processing and printing picking lists • Printing order confirmations • Releasing stock locations • Updating transaction history The units of work, for example records to be processed, are stored in queues within work tables until the corresponding auto start job has processed them. The autostart jobs automatically shut down after hours, but new instances of these jobs start when necessary. Run modes Two run modes exist for auto start jobs: Classic or In-memory Queue (IMQ) which differ on the queuing process. These modes are indicated for each job on ‘Subsystem Job. Open’ (MNS051/E). M3 Applicat

cards.question: Page 51 in 'Job Management' (Auto Start Jobs and Subsystems) - detail 1: Explain '• Classic In this mode, the' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • Classic In this mode, the auto start job runs in a single thread and only one request is processed at a time which is a first in, first out concept. In some auto start jobs, multiple instances are created to enable simultaneous run. Each of these instances has their own single thread. By default, the run modes of auto-start jobs are in Classic mode and it is the users' discretion to modify it and use IMQ. Order entry in (OIS100) OOI950 Auto start job reads the work table T able Program Records written to work table Other tables are written Other programs are called Next unit of work is processed Next unit of work is processed As an example, when customer orders are entered, records are created and stored in the work table. The auto start job continuously monitors the work table and processes the records.

cards.question: Page 51 in 'Job Management' (Auto Start Jobs and Subsystems) - detail 2: Explain 'In the auto start job functionality' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: In the auto start job functionality for M3 Business Engine, you can run parallel jobs against a work table to consume the queue faster. As an alternative, you can set priorities in 'Subsystem Job. Select Record' (MNS052). • IMQ In this mode, the auto start job automatically creates multi-threads to process records depending on the size of the queue. This enables simultaneous execution of work units resulting in better responsiveness of auto start jobs in processing requests. Additional instances of the job will not run if the standard instance of it is running in IMQ mode.

IMQ program the number of records to be distributed to the consumers. Max queue size the maximum number of parallel threads that process jobs on queue. cards.question: Page 53 in 'Job Management' (M3 Business Engine Auto Start Job Descriptions) - detail 1: Explain 'Subsystem control These properties describe the' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Subsystem control These properties describe the auto start jobs: • Job start - The auto start jobs are automatically started each time the subsystem is started. When the subsystem is active, the auto start job functionality is active and works as described in the figure above. • Inactive subsystem - Even when the subsystem is not active, the necessary records are written to the work tables. When the auto start jobs are started again, all records are processed. The records are sorted according to the first in, first out principle.

It is not possible to set up more than one instance of (MMS911) in 'Subsystem Job. Open' (MNS051). If the auto start job 'Continuous net change IMQ' (RPS911) is active, it enables faster MRP calculation processing for items with continuous net change, while ensuring that items are still calculated in the correct low-level code order.

(MMS91 1) Calculate MRP No Planned orders created MRP91 Y es (RPS91 1) Producer (RPS91 1) Consumer N (RPS91 1) Consumer Item data Material plan Forecast Auto allocation - MMS920 The auto allocation process is triggered when a new demand for allocation arise or at goods receipt when there is new stock that can be allocated. The trigging, which can be executed from a number of functions listed in the database model, is done by writing a record to the work table MMM920.

cards.question: Page 66 in 'Job Management' (M3 Business Engine Auto Start Job Descriptions) - detail 2: Explain 'The (MMM920) work table is then' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: The (MMM920) work table is then read by the auto start job 'Auto Allocation' (MMS920) which monitors reservations in the material plan (see (MMS080)) for items that should be automatically allocated. The auto allocation sequence could be by planning date or by order priority and then planning date depending on the setting of 'Auto allocation sequence' (MMS005/G). Auto allocation is performed for reservations inside the demand time fence (see (MMS002/E), (CRS701), and (MMS005/G)) if they are qualified according to the allocation method stated in (MMS002/G).

cards.question: Page 67 in 'Job Management' (M3 Business Engine Auto Start Job Descriptions) - detail 1: Explain 'Further, (MMS920) calls (MMS921) where it' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Further, (MMS920) calls (MMS921) where it is decided what stock to allocate.

cards.question: Page 67 in 'Job Management' (M3 Business Engine Auto Start Job Descriptions) - detail 2: Explain 'In an attempt to simplify the' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: In an attempt to simplify the process, one could say that (MMS920) decides which orders that should get the stock and then (MMS921) is called to decide which balance identity that should be allocated.

cards.question: Page 71 in 'Job Management' (M3 Business Engine Auto Start Job Descriptions) - detail 1: Explain 'Read all corresponding MITPLO items ITNO' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Read all corresponding MITPLO items ITNO included in MITLOC. Key is CONO, PLDT, WHLO and ITNO. If Inspection code QACD= is status set to and MITTRA records are created. If QACD= is a check done against MWOHED, status=90.

cards.question: Page 71 in 'Job Management' (M3 Business Engine Auto Start Job Descriptions) - detail 2: Explain 'Then is status set to and' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Then is status set to and MITTRA records are created. If QACD= is a check first done against MLIAHE, status=70. Then is status set to and MITTRA records are created. There are no input parameters.

cards.goodAnswer: Create MO/PO from Planned Orders - MMS940 The auto start job 'Create MO/PO from Planned Orders' (MMS940) is used to monitor order proposals that are to be converted to purchase orders, manufacturing orders, or distribution orders. Auto start job (MMS940) creates the final orders based on released order proposals. cards.question: Page 101 in 'Job Management' (M3 Business Engine Auto Start Job Descriptions) - detail 2: Explain 'We save the date for the' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: We save the date for the MITPLO record that is being changed, then check after the change if the date/time changed. If it did, then we also check if any links exist (a link can be either an order initiated link or a pre-allocation). If any links exist, then we write a record to table (MMW910) to trigger a rescheduling.

cards.question: Page 102 in 'Job Management' (M3 Business Engine Auto Start Job Descriptions) - detail 1: Explain '(MMS910) checks for the change and' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: (MMS910) checks for the change and calls MMMNGPRR with *RESC if a link exists. MMMNGPRR calls MMMNGPRA with *DATE to write to (MMW910). A number of programs write to (MMW910) directly. This is usually because they update MITPLO directly OR they deal with line type (no MITPLO exists). Write a record to (MMW910). Perform or trigger the reschedule. This is done by (MWS910).

cards.question: Page 102 in 'Job Management' (M3 Business Engine Auto Start Job Descriptions) - detail 2: Explain 'This is an auto start job' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: This is an auto start job. (MWS910) waits for new records to be written into table (MMW910). When a new record arrives, it is processed by (MWS910), then deleted. (MWS910) first checks if the order to be rescheduled has rescheduling turned on (this is a flag on the various order types). If so, then it performs a reschedule according to the type of order. In all cases, notification is done if required according to a parameter passed in (MMW910) (message in M3 Mail). • CO is done in (MWS910) by calling OOLINEPI.

cards.question: Page 102 in 'Job Management' (M3 Business Engine Auto Start Job Descriptions) - detail 3: Explain '• Service orders are done inside' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • Service orders are done inside MWS910 itself. • RO/DO is done inside (MWS910). • MO rescheduling is done by writing a record to (MPM010). Note that we reschedule the start date of the connected operation in all cases except when the acquisition order that triggered the reschedule is a subcontract PO. For subcontract operations, we reschedule using the new date as the finish date. M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Job Management

cards.goodAnswer: Open' (MWS010), parameter Create inbound Delivery is selected. For receipt of DO inbound delivery is needed first when dispatch of DO is done. cards.question: Page 107 in 'Job Management' (M3 Business Engine Auto Start Job Descriptions) - detail 3: Explain 'If it is found that the' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: If it is found that the deadline date/time has passed, then the manual connect flag is changed to 2. When this occurs, if it is found that the lowest status is 60, then the SHIPMENT_ISSUED event is triggered. Process SHIPMENT_ISSUED Event - DRS901 This ASJ processes all occurrences of the SHIPMENT_ISSUED event that have occurred in (DRS900). M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Job Management

cards.goodAnswer: Updated tables DescriptionTable Change workloadMPM960RR Related ASJs and functions DescriptionName NoneASJ: Remove workloadPMS910Functions: Calculate remaining time on MO operation PMS970 Re-create workloadPMS991 Transactions from GLR to POM - POS950 The autostart job 'Transactions from GLR to POM' (POS950) transfers transactions from the table BPO950 to Project Order Management. The table BPO950 contains accounting transactions from General Ledger (GLR) created from program (GLS040). The transactions affect Outcome, Forecast, and Committed amounts and quantities per project in POM/PJM.

cards.question: Page 126 in 'Job Management' (M3 Business Engine Auto Start Job Descriptions) - detail 2: Explain 'The records in the BPO950 table' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: The records in the BPO950 table are created under the following conditions: POM/PJM is installed (installation parameter MNS100/H, PO=1). The transaction is not a recognition accounting transaction (Accounting event PO30). Project and element number exist in the accounting dimensions given by parameters in 'Settings - Project Management' (CRS590).

cards.question: Page 128 in 'Job Management' (M3 Business Engine Auto Start Job Descriptions) - detail 2: Explain 'Through field 240-'Delivery schedule interval' and' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Through field 240-'Delivery schedule interval' and field 255-'Latest received delivery schedule', (RSS950) determines if a delivery schedule from a specific partner is overdue. If this is the case, an application message is sent informing M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Job Management

cards.question: Page 129 in 'Job Management' (M3 Business Engine Auto Start Job Descriptions) - detail 1: Explain 'the responsible user about the situation' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: the responsible user about the situation. The application message sent is 507-'Expected delivery schedule not received' and it has to be enabled in 'Settings - Application Messages' (CRS424) and in 'Partner. Define Application Messages' (RSS017). The application message can be monitored through 'Application Message.

cards.question: Page 129 in 'Job Management' (M3 Business Engine Auto Start Job Descriptions) - detail 2: Explain 'Open' (CRS420). Once a new delivery' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Open' (CRS420). Once a new delivery schedule is received through the M3 EDI Solution, field 255-'Latest received delivery schedule' is updated with date and timestamp. The message flag indicating that a message has been sent is also reset. Once this update is performed, (RSS950) will once again monitor the partner and send a new application message when the number of days and hours in field 240-'Delivery schedule interval' has passed.

cards.question: Page 133 in 'Job Management' (M3 Business Engine Administrator's Guide for Batch Jobs and Job Queues) - detail 1: Explain 'Update in M3 BE database SPS900' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
