The verification process for locked records added in (MMS200) is performed when you go directly from (MMS200) to a detail panel (for example, the E panel) in (MMS001), (MMS002) or (MMS003), and the record locking functionality has been enabled for the applicable program in (CMS005).

cards.question: Page 270 in 'Security Administration' (Record Locking) - detail 2: Explain 'This applies even if (MMS200) does' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: This applies even if (MMS200) does not exist in (CMS005) and therefore cannot be enabled there. Define reason codes It is mandatory to specify a reason code for every locked record. The reason codes are defined in 'Reason Codes. Open' (CMS432). They are used as a selection criterion, and also in the information message that informs a user why a record is locked from changes. This table shows the predefined reason codes and their purpose: PurposeTransaction For records that are locked by an ongoing workflow in ION. 1='Workflow' For records that are locked because of a pending e- signature. 2='E-signature' When an exception occurs that requires you to lock a record manually and not as part of an automated process.

cards.question: Page 270 in 'Security Administration' (Record Locking) - detail 3: Explain 'For this alternative the user ID' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: For this alternative the user ID of the user who locked the record is automatically specified as the external reference connected to the locked record. 3='Manual' There is no built-in automation for workflows or e-signatures, only recommendations of how the reason codes are to be used. The predefined codes cannot be changed or deleted, but name and description can be specified in alternate languages in 'Language Handling. Open' (CRS830) by clicking 'Language' in (CMS432). Reason codes - can be user-defined if more alternatives are required. M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Security Administration

cards.question: Page 271 in 'Security Administration' (Record Locking) - detail 1: Explain 'The records in (CMS432), CERECD table' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: The records in (CMS432), CERECD table, can also be managed through API CMS432MI (Reason code interface). Lock and unlock records Records that should be locked from being edited or deleted are specified in 'Locked Records. Open' (CMS430). Only one record can exist in (CMS430) with the same table and key string, so a record can only be locked once at a time. Locked records are unlocked by deleting them from (CMS430), as the number of records may otherwise grow over time. If you only want to unlock a record temporarily, the parameter 'Lock' can be set to and then changed back to again.

cards.question: Page 271 in 'Security Administration' (Record Locking) - detail 2: Explain 'The related options 20='Lock' and 21='Unlock'' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: The related options 20='Lock' and 21='Unlock' can be used for this purpose or it can be done using corresponding API transactions. This table describes the different fields in (CMS430) and how they are used: DescriptionField The field indicates the name of the table where the locked record is stored. Table The field indicates several key value pairs (field name and a value) which uniquely identify a record in the specified table. In (CMS430) the key values for a record are automatically converted to a key string in URL encoded format, since this is how the key values are stored in the CELOCK table when creating a new locked record. Key string The field indicates the reason why a record is locked and must be specified when creating a locked record. The following alternatives are predefined: 1='Workflow' 2='E-signature' 3='Manual' For alternative 3, the user ID of the person who locked or unlocked the record in the CELOCK table is automat

cards.question: Page 271 in 'Security Administration' (Record Locking) - detail 3: Explain 'See Define reason codes for more' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: See Define reason codes for more information. Reason code The field indicates the identity of a reference con- nected to the locked record. For example, a workflow ID of a workflow started in Infor ION or an e-signature number of an e-signature record. The reference could also be the user ID of the user who locked or unlocked the record in (CMS430) if reason code 3- 'Manual' was selected. External reference M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Security Administration

cards.question: Page 272 in 'Security Administration' (Record Locking) - detail 1: Explain 'DescriptionField The field indicates if a' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: DescriptionField The field indicates if a record is locked from being edited or deleted in the applicable program. = Unlocked 1= Locked Note: The program where this record can be edited must be enabled for record locking in (CMS005) for this parameter to have effect. Lock The field indicates the name and description of the key fields, which is displayed according to the prima- ry key for the specified table. Field / Name The field indicates the key value connected to each key field for a record in the specified table. The val- ues must correspond to an existing record in the specified table to create the record in (CMS430). 'Company' (CONO) is set as default to current com- pany for the logged-on user, if applicable for the ta- ble. Guidance of how to specify different type of values: • If a value in the key is a date this must be speci- fied in the database format YYYYMMDD.

cards.question: Page 272 in 'Security Administration' (Record Locking) - detail 2: Explain '• If a value in the' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • If a value in the key is a decimal field, the deci- mal separator must be specified as a dot '.' Value In (CMS430) manual record locks are specified. For example, to lock template records. Example – Locking a template You want to lock a supplier template from being edited or deleted in 'Supplier. Open' (CRS620). Start (CMS430). Browse (F4) in the field 'Table' in the list and select table CIDMAS because that is the main table connected to (CRS620). Select option 1='Create'.

cards.question: Page 272 in 'Security Administration' (Record Locking) - detail 3: Explain 'On the E panel, select 3-'Manual'' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: On the E panel, select 3-'Manual' in the 'Reason code' field (if no specific reason code used for templates exists). For reason code 3, the 'External ref' field is left blank, otherwise a reference of your choice can be specified in that field. Select the ‘Lock’ check box. Specify the name of your supplier, for example TEMPSUP-US, in the 'Value' field connected to the Field 'IDSUNO'. Click 'Next' to save and lock the record. Example – Unlock template You want to unlock a locked supplier template so it can be edited or deleted. M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Security Administration

cards.question: Page 273 in 'Security Administration' (Record Locking) - detail 1: Explain 'Start (CMS430). Locate the record that' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Start (CMS430). Locate the record that you want to unlock. Select option 4='Delete' and click 'Next' to confirm. If you only want a temporary unlocking and still keep the record in (CMS430), select related option 21='Unlock' instead. Lock and unlock records through API Locking records is usually done through API in an automated process, for example, a workflow in ION. Steps must then be added to the workflow to lock and unlock the records.

cards.question: Page 273 in 'Security Administration' (Record Locking) - detail 2: Explain 'The API CMS430MI (Record locking interface)' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: The API CMS430MI (Record locking interface) has been created to manage records in (CMS430), for the CELOCK table. This table shows the transactions available in CMS430MI: DescriptionTransaction Adds a locked recordAddRecordLock Deletes multiple records from table CELOCKDltMultiRecLock Deletes a locked recordDltRecordLock Gets data for a locked recordGetRecordLock Lists data for locked recordsLstRecordLock Sets a record to lockedSetLock Sets a record to unlockedSetUnlock Updates data for a locked recordUpdRecordLock Note: In the API, the key values must be specified in the 'Key string' field as a bookmark key string in URL encoded format UTF-8. Only one record can exist in the CELOCK table with the same table and key string. But if a record is unlocked (BLOC = 0) this record can be taken over by another external reference and reason code, and set to locked with the 'AddRecordLock' transaction. We recommend that you clear the CELOCK table

cards.question: Page 273 in 'Security Administration' (Record Locking) - detail 3: Explain 'These are descriptions of some of' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: These are descriptions of some of the fields in the transaction and how they are used: • 'To change date' is mandatory and records prior or equal to this are deleted. • If 'Table' is not specified, records for all tables are deleted. • If 'Allow delete when blocked from edit' is left blank, only unlocked records (BLOC = 0) are deleted. • If ADWB is set to 1, even locked records (BLOC = 1) are deleted. • The number of deleted transactions is displayed as output for the transaction. M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Security Administration

cards.question: Page 274 in 'Security Administration' (Security Aspects of M3 Business Engine Functions) - detail 1: Explain 'Security Aspects of M3 Business Engine' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Security Aspects of M3 Business Engine Functions This section describes the attributes of M3 Business Engine functions that relate to security. Use 'Function. Open' (MNS110) to access the definitions of menus and functions within M3 Business Engine. Note: M3 BE considers a menu to be a special type of function. A menu has many of the characteristics of an 'executable' function, such as having the need to be secured, or the capability of being attached to another menu. Creating a menu is done by creating a function with the category MNU. These are the attributes of a function that relate to security: • Function A function consists of an ID and a name. A function is in the menu and you start the program by specifying the function. The function ID is often the same as the ID of the function/program you start, but it does not have to be. You can, for example, name the function (MMS001) as 'ITEMS' in the function ID field. It is the value in

cards.question: Page 274 in 'Security Administration' (Security Aspects of M3 Business Engine Functions) - detail 2: Explain 'You can name (MMS200) "My Item' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: You can name (MMS200) "My Item Register" with the function ID "ITEMS". For your function to start (MMS200), specify 'MMS200' in the 'Program' field. • Function category There are several categories of functions: those that actually run programs, menus, text functions (which are used to place remarks or comments on menus), function groups, and functions that change to a different company or division. Although you can apply security to most of these categories of functions, only two categories have a specific role to play in M3 BE Security: the categories GRP and MNU. • Function category GRP A function with a category of GRP is a function group. Other functions can be placed into a function group. Groups are used to simplify security maintenance by reducing the number of entries made to the list of authorized functions/users. A function with the category GRP cannot be placed in other groups. That is, you cannot have function groups within

cards.question: Page 274 in 'Security Administration' (Security Aspects of M3 Business Engine Functions) - detail 3: Explain 'Open' (MNS110) to attach menu options' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Open' (MNS110) to attach menu options to the menu. A function with the category MNU can be placed into other MNU functions. That is, you can have MNU groups within MNU groups. This is also done by selecting option 12. • PIN code check Before the function runs, the user must specify the correct PIN code. This prevents unauthorized users from running a function at an unattached panel. • Authorization required On 'Function. Open' (MNS110/E), the 'Authorization required' field has these settings: • Not selected (0) means that authorization for the function is checked according to the entries made in 'Authorization by User. Display' (SES401) for the user. If no entries exist in 'Authorization by User. Display' (SES401) for the user, the function is considered approved and open. This means that the system is open for all users if no entries are made in SES400/401, but can be partly limited for some user, user/groups by adding records in SES40

cards.question: Page 275 in 'Security Administration' (Security Audit) - detail 1: Explain '• Selected (1) means that authorization' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • Selected (1) means that authorization for the function is checked according to the entries made in 'Authorization by User. Display' (SES401) for the user. If no entries exist in 'Authorization by User. Display' (SES401) for the user, the function is considered denied and restricted. This means that the system is closed for all users if no entries are made in SES400/401, but can be opened in selected areas and functions for user, user/groups by adding records in SES400/ for these user/groups. The default setting for M3 Business Engine is inactivated (0) for all functions, except for archiving functions where authority set up is required. Note: To restrict access in all company and divisions for a specific function, you should activate the 'Authorization required' field on 'Function. Open' (MNS110/E) for all functions you made entries in 'Function. Connect Authorization by Role' (SES400). To mass change the value for all functions (exce

cards.question: Page 275 in 'Security Administration' (Security Audit) - detail 2: Explain '• Program The 'Program' field is' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • Program The 'Program' field is the actual program that should be started when you specify the current function ID. The function consists of an ID and a name. A function is in the menu and you start the program by specifying the function. The function ID is often the same as the ID of the function/program you start, but it does not have to be. You can, for example, name the function (MMS001) as 'ITEMS' in the function ID field. It is the value in the 'Program' field that determines what program is connected to 'your' function ID. The function name is specified on 'Function. Open' (MNS110/F). You can name (MMS200) "My Item Register" with the function ID "ITEMS." For your function to start (MMS200), specify 'MMS200' in the 'Program' field. Security Audit A security audit table tracks the changes in user permissions caused by certain security changes in M3 Business Engine. Programs used for security audit purposes are 'Security Audit.

cards.question: Page 275 in 'Security Administration' (Security Audit) - detail 3: Explain 'Open' (SES510), 'Security Audit. Print' (SES511)' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Open' (SES510), 'Security Audit. Print' (SES511), and 'Security Audit. Archive/Delete' (SES590). Display security audit records in (SES510) If there are modifications in the authorization setup for any user, (SES510) allows traceability of the security changes on a company level. These are some of the triggers for tracing security changes: • Changes to, creation, or deletion of roles in 'Roles. Open' (MNS405) • User-role connection in 'Roles per User. Connect' (MNS410) • Role-function connection in 'Function. Connect Authorization by Role' (SES400) • User access to company or division in 'User. Access per Company Division' (MNS151) • User settings in 'User. Open' (MNS150) M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Security Administration

cards.question: Page 276 in 'Security Administration' (Security Audit) - detail 1: Explain 'Note: Audit entries are only recorded' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Note: Audit entries are only recorded if there is a movement in 'Authorization per User. Display' (SES401), or when autojob SES900 is started. Also, security changes made using 'Authorization per User. Re-create' (SES990) are not covered by this functionality, and are not logged in the security audit table. On (SES510/E) the authorization values are displayed before and after the security change was made. Specifically, it displays the change in permissions for all options and function keys for a specific user and in which program, company, and division. On (SES510/F), further details of the transaction are displayed. This table describes each field on that panel: DescriptionField heading The field indicates the change ID of the user who created the audit transaction.

cards.question: Page 276 in 'Security Administration' (Security Audit) - detail 2: Explain 'Changed by The field indicates the' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Changed by The field indicates the transaction program used to trigger the permission change. Trans program The field indicates how to classify the type of change that has been made. Note: The first two (2) digits identify the type of record where the transaction was performed: • - Role • - Role/User • - Role/Function • - User/Company/Division • - User. The third digit identifies the type of transaction: • - Auto-create permissions • - Create or copy • - Change • - Delete. Op Code The field indicates the role that has been modified by the transaction, if any. Note: Field is blank if no role was used. Changed role The field indicates the function modified to trigger the transaction, if any. Note: Field is blank if no function was used.

cards.question: Page 276 in 'Security Administration' (Security Audit) - detail 3: Explain 'Changed fnc The field indicates the' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Changed fnc The field indicates the user record that has been modified to trigger the transaction, if any. Note: Field is blank if no user record was used. Changed user Also, SES510MI is used to handle API transactions such as get security audit and list security audit. The first transaction is used to return a single security audit record while the latter returns a list of records matching the input data. The input data used are transaction date (TRDT), transaction time (TRTM), user ID (USIU), company (CONU), division (DIVU), and name of the changed program (PRGNU). M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Security Administration

cards.question: Page 277 in 'Security Administration' (Security Audit) - detail 1: Explain 'Printing security audit records in (SES511)' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Printing security audit records in (SES511) 'Security Audit. Print' (SES511) is used to generate print file SES512PF for the audit records in (SES510). Criteria for the printing selection can be specified. For example, the transaction dates covered, operation code, and change ID. You can also specify if the printout should include records with changes in the basic options, related options, function keys, or a combination. In summary, there is one selection field for each of the three. Each field can be set to 'YES', 'NO', or '-BLANK'.

cards.question: Page 277 in 'Security Administration' (Security Audit) - detail 2: Explain 'For example, set field selection for' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: For example, set field selection for 'basic options' to 'YES' if the printout should include audit records with changes in basic options. If it is set to 'NO', only the audit records without any change in basic options are included. If the field is set to '-BLANK', all audit records are included in the printout. Archive or delete security audit records in (SES590) 'Security Audit. Archive/Delete' (SES590) is used to archive and delete records from the security audit table. On (SES590/E), criteria for archiving can be selected. For example, transaction date, operation code, and change ID.

cards.question: Page 277 in 'Security Administration' (Security Audit) - detail 3: Explain 'If a selection field is left' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: If a selection field is left blank, it is not considered for filtering of the records when the archiving job SES590Sbm is run. A list of archived records can also be printed as printer file SES593PF if the 'Print changes' check box has been enabled on (SES590/E). If the records have been permanently deleted, the printout only contains the primary key values of the record. All the other information has been removed from the database. M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Security Administration

cards.question: Page 278 in 'System Maintenance Run' (Enabling System Maintenance Run) - detail 1: Explain 'Chapter 4: System Maintenance Run Enabling' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Chapter 4: System Maintenance Run Enabling System Maintenance Run This document describes the required settings to enable System Maintenance Runs. Before you start • The subsystem ASJ - 'Subsystem for M3 data base updating must be defined in 'Subsystem. Open' (MNS050). You can create ASJ by select F14='Create standard'. • CSCHJOB 'Scheduled Job Driver' and CMNGJOB 'Batch Job Driver' must be connected to the auto-job subsystem in 'Subsystem Job. Open' (MNS051). • 'System Maintenance. Open' (SHS100) has to be set up as a scheduled job.

cards.question: Page 278 in 'System Maintenance Run' (Enabling System Maintenance Run) - detail 2: Explain 'If you want to include Engineering' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: If you want to include Engineering Change Order (ECS435) in System Maintenance Run, you must activate the ' Engineering change order' field on (MNS100/J). Follow these steps Proceed with the following steps to enable System Maintenance Run. If you don't want to schedule the System Maintenance run, start 'System Maintenance. Open' (SHS100) and select option 9='Run'. Start 'Job Schedule Category. Open' (SHS050) and specify the job category. This field indicates a category, which can be attached to a function in order to determine which job queue and at what time of day the function can be run. Open the E panel and specify this information: From time - The field indicates the earliest time a job (belonging to a job schedule category) can be scheduled to run.

cards.question: Page 278 in 'System Maintenance Run' (Enabling System Maintenance Run) - detail 3: Explain 'To time - The field indicates' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: To time - The field indicates the latest time a job (belonging to a job schedule category) can be scheduled to run. Start 'Job Schedule Function. Open' (SHS030) and specify SHS100 in the 'Function' field. Leave the 'User' field blank. Open the E panel and specify 'JS allowed' field. These options are available. This function is not allowed to be scheduled. This function can be scheduled or run immediately1 M3 Application Foundation User Guide | System Maintenance Run

cards.question: Page 279 in 'System Maintenance Run' (Enabling System Maintenance Run) - detail 1: Explain 'This function must be scheduled. This' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: This function must be scheduled. This function will be scheduled to run once at the earliest time allowed for function category. In the 'JS category' field you select your job category defined in 'Job Schedule Category. Open' (SHS050). Specify 'SHS998' in the 'Program' field. Specify 'SHS999CL' in the 'Job' field. Before a batch job is submitted, M3 Business Engine standard programs calls program'Job Attributes. Select' (MNS230) to see if there are job attribute overrides. One of the parameters passed to this program is the job name. This job name is used to find a job schedule function and check if the job allows scheduling.

cards.question: Page 279 in 'System Maintenance Run' (Enabling System Maintenance Run) - detail 2: Explain 'If scheduling is allowed, the job' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: If scheduling is allowed, the job scheduling screen (SHS230/E) is displayed. Press Enter. Start 'Job Schedule Program. Open' (SHS031) or start through option 11='Program' from (SHS050/B). Specify (SHS998) in the 'Program' field and then open the E panel and specify Description and the display file SHS998DSP. For further information on job scheduler, refer to M3 Business Engine Administrator's Guide for Job Scheduler on page 143. Start 'System Maintenance. Open' (SHS100) and specify a job identity. This field indicates the user defined identity of the system maintenance run. On the E panel, specify a description for the job.

cards.question: Page 279 in 'System Maintenance Run' (Enabling System Maintenance Run) - detail 3: Explain 'This field indicates Field … the' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: This field indicates Field … the user to be notified about the results of the run. User if the defined user should be notified when the system maintenance run has finished successfully. Message type must be activated to enable re- ceipt of an application message. Notify finished if the defined user should be notified when the system maintenance run has failed. Message type must be activated to enable re- ceipt of an application message. Notify failed if the system maintenance run should generate history records. The history records are used for monitoring and comparing earlier runs in 'System Maintenance Run. Analyze' (SHS300). Record history M3 Application Foundation User Guide | System Maintenance Run

cards.question: Page 280 in 'System Maintenance Run' (Programs Included in System Maintenance Runs) - detail 1: Explain 'This field indicates Field the maximum' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: This field indicates Field the maximum number of hours that the system maintenance run is allowed to run before it is considered to be abnormal. When this number of hours is passed, the person responsible for the system maintenance run will be notified. Message type must be activated to enable re- ceipt of an application message. Time out time Start 'System Maintenance. Open Details' (SHS101), through option 11='Details' on (SHS100/B). (SHS101/B) contains an F14 key that generates the std night run set up. Here, you can define if a program should be included in the night run or not. This can be done by deleting the program or by ticking the check box 'Run function' on (SHS101/E).

cards.question: Page 280 in 'System Maintenance Run' (Programs Included in System Maintenance Runs) - detail 2: Explain 'It is also possible to filter' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: It is also possible to filter the content by using the 'from and to date' fields. Note: Activating the run function on (SHS101/E) is recommended at least for required programs listed in Programs Included in System Maintenance Runs on page to ensure a complete system maintenance run. You can change the sequence of the included programs but it is not recommended. Changing the sequence is done by adding a new record (new sequence) with the program you want to move and deleting the old sequence. Open (SHS100/B) and select option 17='Selections'. This starts 'System Maintenance. Open Selections' (SHS105). Here, you can define the company and division (one or several) where the night run should be performed.

cards.question: Page 280 in 'System Maintenance Run' (Programs Included in System Maintenance Runs) - detail 3: Explain 'Programs Included in System Maintenance Runs' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Programs Included in System Maintenance Runs There are optional and mandatory programs included in a System Maintenance Run. While the mandatory programs keep various parts of the system updated, the optional programs clear redundant data record files and recalculate loads/capacities. Note: Since an M3 Business Engine installation always contains all database files, all System Maintenance Run programs can be run although only parts of M3 Business Engine are in use. That is, you do not have to install all M3 Business Engine applications to be able to run System Maintenance Run. Mandatory programs These programs must be run in an M3 Business Engine System Maintenance Run. Program nameProgram ID Update Agreement info on individualsSAS920 Restart interrupted transactions after PDS001PDS999 Restart interrupted MO issuesPMS999A M3 Application Foundation User Guide | System Maintenance Run

cards.question: Page 281 in 'System Maintenance Run' (Programs Included in System Maintenance Runs) - detail 1: Explain 'Program nameProgram ID Create Action Message' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.

System Maintenance Run (SHS101/E) - Run function must be activated. • Restart Interrupted Transactions after PDS001 (PDS999) If the product structure, processed in 'Product Structure. Open' (PDS001) has been interrupted; (PDS999) restart

cards.question: Page 281 in 'System Maintenance Run' (Programs Included in System Maintenance Runs) - detail 2: Explain 'Restart Interruption aft PDS001' (PDS998), available' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Restart Interruption aft PDS001' (PDS998), available on the AUX menu. System Maintenance Run (SHS101/E) - Run function must be activated. • Restart Interrupted MO Issues (PMS999A) This program restarts interrupted manufacturing order (MO) issues. If an MO issue is interrupted, then program (PMS065) is called to complete the processing. This situation occurs mainly when using back flush materials and the user does not leave 'MO Operation. Reporting' (PMS070) before e.g. the back up starts.

cards.question: Page 281 in 'System Maintenance Run' (Programs Included in System Maintenance Runs) - detail 3: Explain 'The back up routine will terminate' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: The back up routine will terminate all programs in use, including this one, and therefore no triggering is done in order to execute the back flush transactions. These transactions will not process until (PMS999) has run. This restart can also be made in 'Restart Interrupted MO Issues PMS065' (PMS998) available on the AUX menu. System Maintenance Run (SHS101/E) - Run function must be activated. • Create Action Message C2 (PMS966) An action message suggests performing a certain planning action. It is automatically created by the system either at change of date or when change has occurred in the material plan for an item. M3 Application Foundation User Guide | System Maintenance Run

cards.question: Page 282 in 'System Maintenance Run' (Programs Included in System Maintenance Runs) - detail 1: Explain 'The action message should be regarded' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: The action message should be regarded as a signal to the planner. The whole philosophy is based on exception technique, giving signals only for transactions that need action. Therefore the setup of parameters and lead-time relating to planning are of great importance. All action messages are related to time fences, which involves usage of the item lead-time. The C2 message (Released order will be delayed) gives an indication that a released order may be delayed. When calculating if the C2 should be applied, the system considers operations and remaining time to estimate a possible delay. Generally it is generated when a manufacturing order has not started or started operation that cannot be finished in time. To manually create C2 messages for transactions, you must run 'Action Message. Create C2' (PMS965) from the MAP menu. The System Maintenance Run program (PMS966) creates the C2 messages automatically. The following criteria must be f

cards.question: Page 282 in 'System Maintenance Run' (Programs Included in System Maintenance Runs) - detail 2: Explain '• Orders must be qualified for' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • Orders must be qualified for selected facilities/warehouse in the run. • The item uses a planning policy where the parameter Create AM for potential delays in (MMS037/F) is activated. • No other action message is active for the manufacturing order. • The planned start date on the manufacturing order head is earlier than time filter date (today's date minus the number of time filter days on the planning policy). • Any operation on the order with status ≥ and ≤ where the calculated finish date is later than the planned finish date. The calculated finish date is calculated by distributing the remaining time according to the capacity in the capacity calendar for the work center. • System Maintenance Run (SHS101/E) - Run function must be activated. • Create Action Message C2 (MOS966) This program is used to create C2 messages for work orders. The following criteria must be fulfilled for a C2 message. • It must be a released manufacturing o

cards.question: Page 282 in 'System Maintenance Run' (Programs Included in System Maintenance Runs) - detail 3: Explain '• The item uses a planning' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • The item uses a planning policy where the parameter Create AM for potential delays on (MMS037/F) is activated. • No other action message is active for the manufacturing order. • The planned start date on the work order head is earlier than time filter date (today's date minus the number of time filter days on the planning policy). • Any operation on the order with status ≤ (not started) and planned start date earlier than the time filter date. • Any operation on the order with status ≥ and ≤ (started, partly reported) where the calculated finish date is later than the planned finish date. The calculated finish date is calculated by distributing the remaining time according to the capacity in the capacity calendar for the work center. • System Maintenance Run (SHS101/E) - Run function must be activated. • Create Past Time Workload (CPS931) This program moves work center load in past time to Monday of the current week or Monday of the p

cards.question: Page 283 in 'System Maintenance Run' (Programs Included in System Maintenance Runs) - detail 1: Explain '• Update Customer with Invoice Amount' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • Update Customer with Invoice Amount from Ledger (CRS930) This program updates customer records with the number of overdue days, current outstanding, and overdue invoices amounts from the sales ledger. The work flow of the program is as follows: (MOS999S2) reads the whole MITPCC file and selects all serialized items that have a Next calculation date (MCCALD) equal or less than today's date. For every item that is selected a record will be written in MITPCC. This record is updated with a in Continuous net change code (MCCONC). When all the MITPCC records have been read, the program calls (MOS950) that will do the actual SRP calculation. The items that are calculated in (MOS950) are the ones marked with a in MITPCC. These records are deleted prior to calling (MOS950).

Please be aware however, that when you run this program manually, you can choose from batch net change run or regenerative run. Regenerative run means that all work requests below a given status are deleted (with some exceptions) before they are regenerated. When MOS999S2 runs in the System Maintenance Run, it is always in net change mode - never regenerative. • Shut down auto jobs (EVS015) The program (EVS015) shuts down autojobs. cards.goodAnswer: Open Schedule Day' (TMS215), etc. The calculation for an employee is either started from the day before the current day, or it is started from Calculate to date (EACTOD) in CEAMP if EACTOD is less than the date for the day before the current day. The calculation is ended either the current day, or the Calculated to date (EACTOD) id EACTOD is greater than the current day. Calculated to date is shown on the (CRS530/F) panel. When the time calculation begins, all previous calculated times, if any, are first backed up to the start date for the calculation. Then the new calculations starts, and the reported times are checked against each employee's calendar. If there are any employees working night shift the current night, no times will be calculated for those employees for the current night but only for earlier nights. cards.question: Page 289 in 'System Maintenance Run' (System Maintenance Run Overview) - detail 3: Explain '• System Maintenance Run (SHS101/E) -' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • System Maintenance Run (SHS101/E) - Run function must be activated. System Maintenance Run Overview There are several functions in M3 Business Engine that are controlled by date, and when the date changes several checks and updates have to be done. System Maintenance Run in M3 Business Engine executes a number of programs which 'clean up' certain elements of M3 database. Some of the programs are always executed when the System Maintenance Run starts, while others are started optionally. System Maintenance Run and Job Scheduler The M3 Business Engine Job Scheduler is used to run programs on schedule. In order for a function to run on a schedule, M3 Business Engine System Maintenance Run must be defined in the M3 Business Engine Job Scheduler as a scheduled job. M3 Business Engine System Maintenance Run runs a number of predefined programs once each night, while other scheduled jobs in M3 Business Engine Job Scheduler often runs one def

cards.question: Page 290 in 'System Maintenance Run' (System Maintenance Run Overview) - detail 1: Explain 'It is possible to run all' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: It is possible to run all System Maintenance Runs in parallel. Shutting down the auto jobs are not necessary from a data consistency point of view but could be selected if you want to minimize the impact on the System Maintenance Run from other activities in the system. Note: Running MRP with automatic release of proposals with A2 messages where the grouping of, e.g. purchase order proposals might be affected. In case of Multi-Company installation, the M3 Business Engine database must have its own System Maintenance Run job set up and scheduled. Different database System Maintenance Runs can be submitted to run concurrently. It is also possible to select which companies and divisions are to be included in the System Maintenance Run for the database. Ordering a System Maintenance Run A System maintenance run can be ordered by in one of the following ways: • Order system maintenance run by the Job Scheduler. • Order system maintenance run

cards.question: Page 290 in 'System Maintenance Run' (System Maintenance Run Overview) - detail 2: Explain 'This is done in 'Job Schedule' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: This is done in 'Job Schedule Category. Open' (SHS050) and 'Job Schedule. Open' (SHS030). After a System Maintenance Run is scheduled in 'Job Schedule Category. Open (SHS050)' and in 'Job Schedule Function. Open (SHS030)' it needs to get ordered in 'System Maintenance. Open (SHS100)'. When completed, the SHS999CL is added to the CSHCTL file in 'Job Schedule Entry. Update (SHS010)'. The job CSCHJOB 'Scheduled Job Driver' in the auto-job subsystem controls if there are any jobs in the CSHCTL file that should get started. At start time, the job CSCHJOB 'Scheduled Job Driver' in SHS999CL submits the file to CMNGJOB 'Batch Job Driver' which starts the System Maintenance Run. When the System Maintenance Run process is finished, the job SHS999CL is again placed in the CSHCTL table in 'Job Schedule Entry.

Open' (SHS100), information about the night run is stored in 'System Maintenance Run. Analyze' (SHS300). Here you can see the different night runs. Here it is possible to restart a failed night run, via option 9=Restart, starting from the program that failed (or any other program). This will run the selected program and all programs with a higher sequence number. There are two additional inquiry types present also, so that you can compare several runs for one function within one job identity or one function within several job identities. cards.question: Page 292 in 'Accessing Information in M3' (Connect User to User Group) - detail 1: Explain 'Chapter 5: Accessing Information in M3' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Chapter 5: Accessing Information in M3 Create a User Group This document explains how to define a user group. A user group is a group of users that have the same access to files and records in M3. For example, all users who are responsible for price and discount maintenance can be defined as one user group. Follow these steps Start 'User Group. Open' (CRS004) and the B panel is displayed. Specify an identity for the user group you are creating. Press Enter to proceed to the E panel. On the E panel, fill in the user group’s name and description.

Close the program when there are no more user groups to be defined. Outcome A user group is created and it is now possible to connect users to the user group. User groups are saved in the parameter file (CSYTAB). User groups can be used together with object access groups. In this way, only a limited number of people in a user group will have access to, for example, a company’s price lists. This limited number of people forms an object access group.

cards.question: Page 294 in 'Accessing Information in M3' (Create and Configure User Defined Menu) - detail 1: Explain 'On the B panel, set the' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: On the B panel, set the panel sequence. This instruction is based on panel sequence E. Enter an identity for the group. Press New. The identity can be alpha-numeric and consist of up to ten characters. On the E panel, enter a description and a name. Press Enter. Create and Configure User Defined Menu This document describes how to create and configure user specific menu in 'Menu Version. Open' (MNS080). Introduction Whenever a user opens an M3 functionality in M3 H5, the menu is displayed based on the settings in MNS111MI. These settings are user specific and depend on the security settings for the user in M3 Business Engine and also which menu version the user is connected to in 'User.

cards.question: Page 294 in 'Accessing Information in M3' (Create and Configure User Defined Menu) - detail 2: Explain 'Open' (MNS150/E). Follow these steps A' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Open' (MNS150/E). Follow these steps A user, by default, is connected to a blank menu version in 'Function. Open' (MNS110). To create User Defined Menu, follow these steps. Create a new Menu Version Start 'Menu Version. Open' (MNS080). Specify a Menu version and select Options > Create. Specify the name for the new Menu version on the E-panel. Click Next. The new menu version is created. Create a new Menu To create a new Menu for the created Menu version, you can copy and connect existing Menu(s) (and their submenus) used in the default (or any other) menu version.

cards.question: Page 294 in 'Accessing Information in M3' (Create and Configure User Defined Menu) - detail 3: Explain 'Start 'Menu Version. Open' (MNS080). To' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Start 'Menu Version. Open' (MNS080). To copy a menu, right-click a record and select Copy. Specify the Menu version you want to copy to and click Next until you are back on the B-panel again. Repeat these steps until all submenus and menus are connected to the new Menu version that you created. Connect a Menu Version to a User Start 'User. Open' (MNS150). Select the current user and open the F-panel. M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Accessing Information in M3

cards.question: Page 295 in 'Accessing Information in M3' (Connect User Group to Object Access Group) - detail 1: Explain 'Select the menu version that you' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Select the menu version that you created. Menu version is what will be shown in the menu. Click Next and close 'User. Open' (MNS150). Depending on the security settings in 'Function. Connect Authorization by Role' (SES400), your menu version displays the functions your user is connected to. Display the new Menu Log off and log on to M3 H5. After logging in, the new menu items with connected functionality are available. Connect User Group to Object Access Group This document explains how you define the rules for user access to information in M3 by connecting groups of users to object access groups.

cards.question: Page 295 in 'Accessing Information in M3' (Connect User Group to Object Access Group) - detail 2: Explain 'Outcome One or several user groups' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Outcome One or several user groups are connected to an object access group. Connect specific components or accounting identities to the respective object access group to control the information access. The access group file (FACCES) is updated. Before you start An object access group must be created in 'Object Access Group. Open' (CRS006). Follow these steps Start 'Object Access Group. Open' (CRS006). On the B panel, select option 11='User groups' for the object access group to work with. 'Object Access Group.

cards.question: Page 295 in 'Accessing Information in M3' (Connect User Group to Object Access Group) - detail 3: Explain 'Connect User Group' is called. On' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Connect User Group' is called. On panel (CRS007/B), enter the identity of the user group to connect. Press New. On panel (CRS007/E), check the description of the user group to make sure that the right group is selected. Press Enter. To add more user groups to the same object access group, repeat steps on panel (CRS007/B) and (CRS007/E). M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Accessing Information in M3

cards.question: Page 296 in 'Accessing Information in M3' (Defining Access to Information in M3) - detail 1: Explain 'Define Check of Information Access for' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Define Check of Information Access for Financial Programs and Accounting Dimensions This document explains how you set the rules for accessing information in the financial system. Outcome Programs within the financial system are included in the authorization check routine. For each program, the accounting dimensions to be checked are selected. When you start one of the included programs, the accounting identity used for the selected dimension is automatically checked against the object access group in 'Accounting Identity. Open' (CRS630/F) to verify that the user is authorized. The parameter file (CSYTAB), key AACH, is updated. Before you start • Accounting dimension names should be defined in 'Accounting Dimension. Enter Names' (CRS012).

cards.question: Page 296 in 'Accessing Information in M3' (Defining Access to Information in M3) - detail 2: Explain '• The company's chart of account' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • The company's chart of account must be defined in 'Accounting Identity. Open' (CRS630). • Object access groups should be connected to the relevant accounting identities in (CRS630/F). Follow these steps Start 'Settings – Access Authority Check' (GLS005). The B panel is displayed, listing the relevant financial programs with indications on whether they are included in the access check. If you call (GLS005) for the first time, import a standard setup of programs by pressing F14 on the B panel. Select a program with option Open. On the E panel, change the standard description and name, if desired.

cards.question: Page 296 in 'Accessing Information in M3' (Defining Access to Information in M3) - detail 3: Explain 'Select the 'Access check' check box' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Select the 'Access check' check box whether the program should be included in the authorization check. Select the check boxes for the accounting dimensions you wish to be checked when starting the program. Press Enter. Defining Access to Information in M3 This document explains how to define the user access to information in M3 by working with groups of users. Outcome User access to specific components and financial information is defined by having connected user groups to object access groups. The object access groups are connected to specific components and accounting identities. M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Accessing Information in M3

cards.question: Page 297 in 'Accessing Information in M3' (Defining Access to Information in M3) - detail 1: Explain 'Furthermore, financial programs are selected and' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Furthermore, financial programs are selected and included in the automatic authorization check routine. The access definitions are used to ensure that the users can retrieve the information and work with the components relevant to their respective role within the company. When a user tries to access information from these components - or accounting identities - there is an automatic check that the user is connected to the valid user group. Consequently, it is also used to prevent unauthorized persons from monitoring or maintaining specific data in M3. See the respective settings instruction for further information. Before you start See the respective settings instruction for further information on starting conditions. Follow these steps Create user group Begin by creating an empty user group by giving it an identity in 'User Group. Open' (CRS004). The group can be accessed from every company and division.

cards.question: Page 297 in 'Accessing Information in M3' (Defining Access to Information in M3) - detail 2: Explain 'Connect user to user group Connect' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Connect user to user group Connect each user to a user group in 'User. Access per Company Division' (MNS151); the program is called via 'User. Open' (MNS150). A user does not have to be a member of a user group. Note, however, that a single user cannot be connected to more than one group if user groups are used. Create object access group Create an empty group by giving it an identity in 'Object Access Group. Open' (CRS006). The group can be accessed from every company and division. Connect user group to object access group Connect the user group to the object access group in 'Object Access Group.

cards.question: Page 297 in 'Accessing Information in M3' (Defining Access to Information in M3) - detail 3: Explain 'Connect User Group' (CRS007); the program' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Connect User Group' (CRS007); the program is called via (CRS006). If the user group for a user is not connected to an object access group this way, the user does not have access authorization to components or accounting identities with connected object access groups. Connect components to object access group Define who should be permitted to work with specific objects or components by connecting the respective component to the desired object access group. Examples of such components are facility, sales price list, and sales statistics. To limit the user access to specific accounting identities in the financial system, connect the object access groups to any number of such identities in 'Accounting Identity. Open' (CRS630). Note: If no object access group is connected to the component or accounting identity, it has no access security and can be accessed by all users. For a list of programs where an object access group can be entered, see

cards.question: Page 334 in 'Accessing Information in M3' (Object Access Control) - detail 1: Explain 'Object Access Control This document explains' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Object Access Control This document explains what object access control is. It also lists the different programs in Sales and Distribution where this functionality can be implemented. Before you start • User groups, object access groups and the connections between them must be defined before you can begin using object access control. Each user who is to be included must be connected to a user group. • A user must be defined in 'User. Open' (MNS150). • A user group must be defined in 'User Group. Open' (CRS004). • An object access group must be defined in 'Object Access Group. Open' (CRS006) and be connected to a user group in 'Object Access Group. Connect User Group' (CRS007). Purpose Object access control is a security feature for functions in M3.

cards.question: Page 334 in 'Accessing Information in M3' (Object Access Control) - detail 2: Explain 'You can use object access control' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: You can use object access control to ensure that only authorized personnel have access to certain records. When a user tries to access a record that is connected to an object access group, the system checks to see whether the user belongs to a user group that is connected to the relevant object access group. If so, the user is granted access. If not, a message appears on the screen telling the user that he/she has been denied access. Example: Using object access control You have created an object access group called Prices for price maintenance. You have also created a user group called Pricemaint for personnel who are responsible for pricing. All qualified users must be connected to this user group. To make object access control work, the object access group and the user group need to be connected to each other. A price list has to be connected to the object access group Prices in the function 'Sales Price List. Open' (OIS017/E). If a
