cards.goodAnswer: Object access control can be used when a company, for security reasons, wants only a limited number of employees to have access to records in various programs such as price lists. M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Accessing Information in M3

cards.question: Page 336 in 'Output Management' (Output Management Introduction) - detail 1: Explain 'Chapter 6: Output Management Output Management' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Chapter 6: Output Management Output Management Introduction This document describes the output management solution in M3 Business Engine and is intended for administrators who need to set up, manage, administer, and maintain output in M3. Overview • Output to be read by a user The process to generate output to be read by a user is started in M3 Business Engine, but is finalized in another external application. The content is created through M3 Output functions, and then sent to an external output management application for layout design and distribution. A multi-level XML file is used as the output format. The XML file is highly standardized and fully extensible by configuration. Labels (field headings) are sent in the XML file, already translated to correct language (based on user, customer, or supplier).

cards.question: Page 336 in 'Output Management' (Output Management Introduction) - detail 2: Explain 'Numbers and dates follow the XML' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Numbers and dates follow the XML standard. For best performance, standard XML file configuration delivered from Infor only includes the most important fields, but all fields in all M3 BE tables can be made available for the output function and added to the XML file. These are the ways to make additional fields available for the output function: • Add additional field from standard table • Add additional field from table with one-to-many relationship (CMS008) • Add additional field from table with one-to-one relationship (CMS011) • Add virtual field (CMS012) • Add additional field from custom subtotal fields (CMS018) • Add custom text blocks. The main purpose of the XML file is to send it to an external output management application for layout and distribution. The XML file has multiple purposes and can also be used to create XML files used for integration to other systems. • Output to be shared with other systems For output to be shared

cards.question: Page 336 in 'Output Management' (Output Management Introduction) - detail 3: Explain 'Although no content is created, an' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Although no content is created, an initiator with keys to the related information is shared with an output service (Infor Enterprise Collaborator (IEC)). In IEC, content is created by detecting who is the sender, who is the receiver, and which initiator was sent. The correct definition is found for how content should be created and a script is run which uses the keys in the initiator to retrieve the correct information from M3 Business Engine using M3 APIs. For more information about this type of output, see the M3 Business Message Initiator. • Output functions M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Output Management

cards.question: Page 337 in 'Output Management' (Output Management Introduction) - detail 1: Explain 'M3 Output is created by an' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: M3 Output is created by an output function. The output function can be a standalone function which has a purpose of creating M3 Output, for example, 'Inventory Value. Print Reconcil List' (CAS530); or part of a process, for example, 'Supplier Payment Proposal. Create' (APS131). Output can be ordered manually or automatically. Output is initiated when a user logs into M3 BE and manually orders it. The order can be initiated several ways – for example, by selecting an option or using a function key for a business transaction, or by specifying output functions. Automatic output is ordered by auto jobs. How the output is initiated is determined by the output configurations that have been defined. The auto job user is the ordering user. The user and job data is used as the key values for the output definition selection search when the auto job initiates output.

cards.question: Page 337 in 'Output Management' (Output Management Introduction) - detail 2: Explain '• Printer files All settings and' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • Printer files All settings and configuration for M3 output is done per printer file (a technical instance serving as a unique identifier for M3 Output). One M3 Business Engine output function is connected to one Printer file, but you can also connect M3 Business Engine output functions to several printer files. The Printer files are separated into these types: Document Output classified as document is usually intended for external use. The content of the XML includes, in addition to lines, the header, text, address and summary sections. The layout is designed with portrait orientation. Report Output classified as report is usually intended for internal use. The main content of the XML is lines, but it can also include a minor header and summary. The layout is designed with landscape orientation The printer file type is predefined and cannot be changed. The type defines what XML structure format the printer file uses. The format determ

cards.question: Page 337 in 'Output Management' (Output Management Introduction) - detail 3: Explain 'For example, the output function 'Customer' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: For example, the output function 'Customer Invoice. Enter Manual' (ARS120) generates a normal invoice, a credit invoice, or a corrective invoice, where the credit invoice and corrective invoice includes more XML elements than the normal invoice. • Report layout The XML file to be created when running an M3 Business Engine Output function is defined by report layout. You can select different report layouts to be used by the printer file, since it is possible to create different kinds of XML files for the same printer file. The report layout contains the XML structure (the actual XML file configuration) connected to settings that can add additional metadata in the XML file. This metadata is used by external output management applications when converting the XML file to a document or report. • XML structure The XML structure is a central component in M3 output management. It holds all configuration information for the XML file. All metadat

cards.question: Page 338 in 'Output Management' (Output Management Introduction) - detail 1: Explain 'Output selection based on the user' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Output selection based on the user The most common output selection scenario is when the user determines the output. The user can determine the output based on different combinations of M3 application area or printer file and user ID. The combination of these values then determines an output selection. Examples: • A user wants all output to always be printed on the same printer. The ID of that user therefore determines the media selection. The user selects the printer and output server. • A specific document should always be printed on a specific printer. The document therefore determines the media selection. • All documents for an entire application in M3 Business Engine should be printed on a specific printer.

cards.question: Page 338 in 'Output Management' (Output Management Introduction) - detail 2: Explain 'In this case, only the first' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: In this case, only the first two letters of the application area must be set. Output selection based on content In some cases, the output selection must be determined by one or more combinations of content within the output. For example, you may want to send purchase orders to all your suppliers by email. Since each supplier has a different email address, the purchase orders should be automatically sent based on those settings. The receivers and the processing information are then retrieved as content from the output to determine the output selection for each purchase order. Therefore, the settings for each receiver of the purchase order override the settings for the user. Using Infor Document Management (IDM) for document design and distribution Infor Document Management can be used as an application for document design and distribution. M3 Business Engine has a built-in integration to IDM using the application Programming Interface (A

cards.question: Page 338 in 'Output Management' (Output Management Introduction) - detail 3: Explain 'IDM is Infor’s document management solution' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: IDM is Infor’s document management solution which provides a central repository for all business documents across M3 applications. From IDM, you can search for documents based on type or attributes. Configuration regarding what document types, attributes ID, attribute values, and access control lists that should be used in IDM, is carried out in M3 BE settings programs. Infor delivers a template layout package for IDM to be used together with standard M3 BE configuration. Using OpenText StreamServe and Exstream for document design and distribution OpenText StreamServe and OpenText Exstream, a third-party product, can also be used for document design and distribution using the tool StoryTeller. This receives the XML file from M3 and produces the finished document depending on its configuration. This configuration is also called a project. Infor delivers a template StreamServe or Exstream project configuration to be used together with sta

cards.question: Page 339 in 'Output Management' (Define Basic Data for Output Management) - detail 1: Explain '• Save the document to disc' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • Save the document to disc • Send the document for archiving to IDM. Define Basic Data for Output Management This document explains how you define basic data for Output Management. Basic data includes media, output server, output service, and its type. Define settings for Output Management In 'Settings - Output Management' (CMS700), the general settings for output management are defined. - Program used for confirming the Output job The field indicates what program to use if confirmation of output job is selected in 'Output Service Selection. Open' (MNS204). If alternative is selected, 'Output. Select Media' (MNS212) is used to confirm the media settings.

cards.question: Page 339 in 'Output Management' (Define Basic Data for Output Management) - detail 2: Explain 'All media listed in (MNS212) are' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: All media listed in (MNS212) are included in the output job if not manual deleted from the job. If alternative is selected, program (MNS215) is used to confirm the media settings. Only selected media contained in (MNS215) are included in the output job. Selected media is saved per user and used as default for the next output job. - Maximum file size in MB The parameter 'Maximum file size in MB' in (CMS700) is considered when writing to the output file. This function is valid for both XML and CSV file formats. To avoid manual mistakes in this critical configuration, file sizes of at least MB and less than 1000MB ( GB) are validated in the system. If the limit is reached, no more data is written to the output file and the file receives the status MSG in 'Output.

cards.question: Page 339 in 'Output Management' (Define Basic Data for Output Management) - detail 3: Explain 'Manage per File' (MNS209) with message' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Manage per File' (MNS209) with message: 'File size limit of 10MB reached, the file will not be sent.' Note: This file size limit is not applicable for Financial Report. - Document type ID for large input files This field indicates the document type ID, which is used to hold the temporary files needed for processing a large file. The file is considered large if it is more than MB in size. A smaller file is still processed using the old solution through a base64 string. If this field is hidden, the provisioned document type is used from IDM to send the XML or CSV first, and then retrieve it by XQuery to generate a PDF or Excel output regardless of the file size. - Maximum number of archive attributes The field indicates the maximum number of archive attributes that is used as multi-value attributes. M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Output Management

cards.question: Page 340 in 'Output Management' (Define Basic Data for Output Management) - detail 1: Explain 'Email distribution to multiple email addresses' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Email distribution to multiple email addresses using email groups Email distribution to multiple email addresses is completed using an email group. The 'Email group' is an additional field in 'Output Media Selection. Open' (MNS205) and 'Doc Media Control Object. Connect Media' (CRS949) that are used in combination with the 'To email' field. In the XML file, email group information is displayed in the media section wherein 'To email' (TOMA) and 'To email group' (EMGR) information are displayed as one in the TOMA field. All valid email addresses listed in 'Email Group. Open Email Address' (CRS119) receives a copy of the document. These are the email group programs available for email distribution: • 'Email Group. Open' (CRS118) contains email groups (EMGR) that are used in (MNS205), (CRS949), (MNS215), and (MNS212). Upon creation of the email group, the 'No. of Email' (MXRE) field is set to but you have the option to change it up to 100.

cards.question: Page 340 in 'Output Management' (Define Basic Data for Output Management) - detail 2: Explain 'Open Email Address' (CRS119) contains all' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Open Email Address' (CRS119) contains all the email addresses included in the email group. (CRS119) has two email types: • Email type '00' which means the email is manually created and requires email type and email address to create. • Email type '01-99' which gets the information and validation base on the 'Email Address. Open' (CRS111) program. Email type and email key are required to create a record in (CRS119). Additionally, another column address is added to display the email address used. Define printer files In 'List and Printer programs. Configure' (CMS005), all printer files are enabled for XML display. In 'Select' field, select 3-'Printer files' to see all printer files. Printer file type The Printer files are separated into two types: 1-'Document' or 2-'Report' . In (CMS005), you can see the type for a specific printer file. This type is predefined and unalterable. The type affects what XML structure format the printer file c

cards.question: Page 340 in 'Output Management' (Define Basic Data for Output Management) - detail 3: Explain 'Open' (CMS006). Note: Before you start' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Open' (CMS006). Note: Before you start with configuration for a printer file, you should check that the configuration is updated to the latest standard. In 'List and Printer programs. Configure' (CMS005), use related 25='Reset to standard' . Stream files The field indicates if stream files are used as output format for the printer file. Note that output format stream files are not maintained and only provided as is. We recommend that you use XML instead. Global sequence number Select the check box if the read sequence number for 'Related Tables. Connect' (CMS011) and the calculation sequence number for 'Virtual Fields. Connect' (CMS012) is combined, and used as global sequence numbers. M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Output Management

cards.question: Page 341 in 'Output Management' (Define Basic Data for Output Management) - detail 1: Explain 'If global sequence number is activated' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: If global sequence number is activated in (CMS005), the button 'Global seq nr' appears in 'XML Structure Section. Open Element' (CMS009). By clicking the button, the program 'Global sequence number. Display' (CMS014) is started. Note: If the check box is not selected, all related tables are read before any virtual field is calculated. Interface This field indicates what output is generated. You can select one of these options: • 0-'Always XML': Display the printer file into PDF and Word format. • 1-'Override is Allowed': Display output based on what is defined in 'Output Media Selection. Open' (MNS205), 'Output. Select Media' (MNS215), or (MNS212) upon confirmation.

cards.question: Page 341 in 'Output Management' (Define Basic Data for Output Management) - detail 2: Explain '• 2-'Always CSV': Display printer files' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • 2-'Always CSV': Display printer files in Excel format. Archive control This field indicates how archiving settings for a printer file is controlled. Select 0-'Use setting in CMS006' to use the archive settings in (CMS006). You can select one of these options: • 1-'Yes': Use the archive settings in (CMS005). • 2-'No': Disable the archive settings in (CMS005) and use (MNS205) or 'Doc Media Control Object. Connect Media' (CRS949) media. If the 'Update version' option is enabled for Archive Control 1, 2, or 3, the version of a stored document in IDM will be updated if it matches the archiving attributes and document type of the new document. This functionality is exclusive to IDM. Archive If the 'Archive control' selected is 1-'Yes', this field is displayed to indicate if copies of the printer file are archived for document type printer files. You can select one of these options: • 1-'Only original': Archives only the original document.

cards.question: Page 341 in 'Output Management' (Define Basic Data for Output Management) - detail 3: Explain '• 2-'Only copies': Archives only the' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • 2-'Only copies': Archives only the copies of document. • 3-'Both original and copies': Archives both the original and copies of document. File type If the 'Archive control'selected is 1-'Yes' for both document and report type printer files in (CMS005), this field is displayed to indicate the archived file type. If IDM Output Management is used for Interface XML, the only valid alternatives are PDF, Word, or Word and PDF. Selecting any of the other alternative results in the file type, PDF. If IDM Output Management is used for Interface CSV, all alternatives are ignored, and the file type is always Excel. When archiving documents in IDM, the valid file types are 1-'PDF', 2-'Excel', and 6-'Word'. The 'Update version' option is not valid for file type 7-'Word and PDF'. Multi-value attribute This field indicates if you should use an archive as a multi-value archive attribute when saving an output M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Out

cards.question: Page 342 in 'Output Management' (Define Basic Data for Output Management) - detail 1: Explain 'Generate standard To generate a standard' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Generate standard To generate a standard configuration for printer files and basic data programs, select function key F14='Gen standard'. The N panel is displayed. Printer files Select the check box to generate standard configuration for all printer files. Check box must be blank if the field printer file is selected. Printer file Indicates if standard configuration should be generated for a specific printer file. Must be blank if field printer files are selected. List programs Select the check box to generate standard configuration for all List programs. List program Indicates if standard configuration should be generated for a specific List program. This must be blank if field List programs are selected. Basic data for Output Management Select the check box to generate standard basic data for output management.

cards.question: Page 342 in 'Output Management' (Define Basic Data for Output Management) - detail 2: Explain 'Note that this data is shared' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Note that this data is shared between all companies. These programs are updated: • 'Output Service Type. Open' (MNS217) is updated with service type M3_XML, interface is set to XML. Only a new record is added, any existing record remains unchanged. • 'Output Service Type. Connect Media' (MNS214) is updated with service types M3_XML connected to Media MAIL, PRT, and ARCHIVE. Only new records are added, any existing records are not changed. • 'Document Type. Open' (MNS060) is updated with the document types below. Only new records are added; any existing records remain unchanged.

cards.question: Page 342 in 'Output Management' (Define Basic Data for Output Management) - detail 3: Explain 'AttributeDocument type IDDescriptionDocument type M3_Printer_File =' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: AttributeDocument type IDDescriptionDocument type M3_Printer_File = Cur- rent value M3_Layout_TemplateM3 Default Layout Tem- plate M3_TEMPLATE_DEF M3_Printer_File = Cur- rent value M3_LayoutM3 Layout DefaultM3_DEF 'Output Service. Open' (MNS216) is updated with three service IDs which are categorized into two different communication types. Communication type - IDM Output Management, Single Interface for M3_IDM_XML and M3_IDM_CSV service IDs. • Description is set to IDM Output Management. • Service type is set to M3_XML (for service ID M3_IDM_XML). • Service type is set to M3_CSV (for service ID M3_IDM_CSV). • Priority is set to M3_DEF. • Priority is set to M3_TEMPLATE_DEF. M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Output Management

cards.question: Page 343 in 'Output Management' (Define Basic Data for Output Management) - detail 1: Explain 'Communication type - IDM Output Management' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Communication type - IDM Output Management, Double Interface for M3_IDM_COM. • Description is set to IDM Output Management. • No service type is defined. • Interface XML and Interface CSV are created. At least one of the Priority has a value for the Interfaces: • Priority is set to M3_DEF. • Priority is set to M3_TEMPLATE_DEF. Only a new record is added; any existing records remain unchanged. Output selection based on user Select the check box to generate standard output selection data based on user. 'Output Service Selection.

cards.question: Page 343 in 'Output Management' (Define Basic Data for Output Management) - detail 2: Explain 'Open' (MNS204) is updated with a' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Open' (MNS204) is updated with a record for 'Blank Printer file' and 'Blank User'. Service ID is set to M3_IDM. Only new records are added; any existing records are never changed. Note: Records are created for all divisions in the current company. Update to standard for printer files Select the check box to update all standard fields for existing printer files in (CMS005). Update is only done for standard structure and retains customization of printer files. Reset and Update standard To Reset to Standard or Update to Standard for Printer files and basic data program, select Reset/Updat Std from the Actions field or press the F15. Then, the S panel is displayed. In the S panel, 'From' and 'To' values are defined in the Selection Criteria.

cards.question: Page 343 in 'Output Management' (Define Basic Data for Output Management) - detail 3: Explain 'Options from the Reset/Updat std field' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Options from the Reset/Updat std field in Operations are 0-'Reset to standard' and 1-'Update standard'. Define printer file variants Variants per printer file are defined in 'Printer File Variants. Open' (CMS004). Printer file variants are predefined and can be modified according to selection. Selection in naming printer file variants There are different ways to set how printer file variants should be named: • Alternative No label is included. • Alternative - Standard label The standard M3 message ID is used from the table where the field originates. Translation is taken from message file MVXCON. • Alternative - Overriding message M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Output Management

cards.question: Page 344 in 'Output Management' (Define Basic Data for Output Management) - detail 1: Explain 'An overriding M3 message ID and' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: An overriding M3 message ID and message file can be specified. If no message file is specified, MVXCON is used. • Alternative - Overriding name An overriding text can be specified. The overriding text can be 1- characters long. Translation is specified by using the language button. The check box 'Fallback to standard msg' is used when the language set for the printer file is not specified in the language button. If the check box is activated, 'Standard message ID' is used as a label in the XML for a field, section and variant. If the check box is not activated, XML label of a field, section or variant will be no label. Define report layouts Report Layout is defined in 'Report Layout.

cards.question: Page 344 in 'Output Management' (Define Basic Data for Output Management) - detail 2: Explain 'Open' (CMS025). The definition consists of' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Open' (CMS025). The definition consists of the XML structure and settings that can add additional metadata in the XML file. This metadata is used by external output management applications when converting the XML file to a document or report. Standard report layouts are delivered for every printer file variant using naming standard M3_STD_0y-0x (y=variant, x=sequence no). Report layouts starting with M3 are reserved for standard reports, and cannot be created or deleted. Watermark A watermark is added to individual printer files in 'Report Layout. Open' (CMS025) or to all printer files in 'System Settings. Open' (MNS090). The text to be used as a watermark is included in the XML file in different ways: • Alternative - No watermark text is included.

cards.question: Page 344 in 'Output Management' (Define Basic Data for Output Management) - detail 3: Explain '• Alternative - M3 Business Engine' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • Alternative - M3 Business Engine message ID used as watermark text. If no message file is specified, MVXCON is automatically selected. • Alternative - A watermark text is specified. The watermark may be disabled, that is, not included in XML file based on a virtual field from the section type REPORT. If the virtual field is true (value 1), the field is disabled. Select report layout for the output function In 'Report Layout Selection. Open' (MNS210) the report layout is connected to a printer file variant and user. The following read logic is applied when running an output function until a record is found in (MNS210): UserDivisionSequence XX01 BlankX02 M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Output Management

cards.question: Page 345 in 'Output Management' (Define Basic Data for Output Management) - detail 1: Explain 'UserDivisionSequence XBlank03 BlankBlank04 Define document type' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: UserDivisionSequence XBlank03 BlankBlank04 Define document type The document type is defined in 'Document type. Open' (MNS060). The definition is used to build a specific search query for a file within IDM that holds a document design. In 'Output Service. Open' (MNS216) a search sequence can be defined with a maximum of six documents types. Document type ID Indicates a document type ID within IDM. The ID in M3 BE must exactly match the ID in IDM. Browse and validation is done against IDM.

cards.question: Page 345 in 'Output Management' (Define Basic Data for Output Management) - detail 2: Explain 'Attribute ID Indicates an attribute ID' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Attribute ID Indicates an attribute ID within IDM. A valid attribute ID is dependent on a document type ID. The ID in M3 BE must exactly match the ID in IDM. Attribute value Indicates how to set the value for an attribute ID. Multi-value attribute This check box indicates if a document type attribute is a multi-value attribute. When a multi-value attribute ID is enabled in M3, you must also enable the equivalent configuration in Infor Document Management (IDM) for the attribute. You must align the settings in IDM and M3 for multi-value attributes when configuring this feature. Define output service type In 'Output Service Type.

cards.question: Page 345 in 'Output Management' (Define Basic Data for Output Management) - detail 3: Explain 'Open' (MNS217), output service types are' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Open' (MNS217), output service types are defined which determine the output format (interface): • XML Multi-level XML files are highly standardized and fully extensible by configuration. Field from all tables in M3 Business Engine are available. Labels (field headings) are sent in the XML file, already translated to correct language (based on user, customer or supplier). Numbers and dates follow the XML standard. • Stream file Stream files are text files with a name-value format intended to be used by external output management applications. They contain these sections: • Media control section - where you can specify multiple media and control the required output. M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Output Management

cards.question: Page 346 in 'Output Management' (Define Basic Data for Output Management) - detail 1: Explain '• Front-page section - where you' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • Front-page section - where you can specify the document output ordering data, and the selection criteria to be used for the output. • Document data - where the document data is grouped by a set of records with multiple detailed lines. Note: Stream files are not maintained and only provided as is. We recommend that you use XML instead. • XMLOUT XMLOUT is intended for use when sharing output with other systems. XMLOUT is a stream file, although it is in XML format. Compared to STREAM, the XMLOUT file only contains control information and keys to the business data, instead of declaring the full set of data. For more information about the XMLOUT format, see the M3 Business Message Initiator. • CSV This output service type is intended for the use of External Output Management applications.

cards.question: Page 346 in 'Output Management' (Define Basic Data for Output Management) - detail 2: Explain 'CSV files are text files with' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: CSV files are text files with delimiter separated values. You can change the default delimiter, which is comma (,). You can select an escape sequence character at the line end as part of the CSV output interface feature. These options are valid for CSV: • 1-[LF]: Use line feed to start a new line but keep the cursor where it is. • 2-[CR][LF]: Use carriage return line feed to start a new line and send the cursor back to the beginning. • FLAT FILE This output service type is intended to generate a plain text file that does not contain any structured relationships with other files or databases. This is not supported when using communication type - 'Single interface' and - 'Double interface'. 'Line ending' field in 'Output Service Type. Open' (MNS217) is also used in Flat file.

cards.question: Page 346 in 'Output Management' (Define Basic Data for Output Management) - detail 3: Explain 'Connect media to service type To' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Connect media to service type To connect a Service type to Media, use 'Output Service Type. Connect Media' (MNS214). Define service identity Use 'Output Service. Open' (MNS216) to define the service identity (ID). The service ID identifies an external application or output server used to process output generated by M3 BE. Service type Every service ID is connected to a service type that defines the output format for the service. For that reason, one service ID can only support one single output format. Communication type The Communication type defines the technical solution to communicate to the external application or output server. • TCP/IP connector M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Output Management

cards.question: Page 347 in 'Output Management' (Define Basic Data for Output Management) - detail 1: Explain 'The field is sent through a' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: The field is sent through a TCP/IP socket connection to an output server. Technically, the connector is defined as a port at a host, which is the output server. • Infor Reporting event An Infor reporting event is created and sent to M3 Event Hub when the file has been created. • Transfer files The file is placed in M3 Transfer Files. • IDM Output Mgmt, single interface The file is sent to IDM Output Management using IDM’s Application Programming Interface (API). A search sequence with a maximum of six document types is defined. • Amazon S3 bucket An Amazon S3 bucket can be used to transfer large XML files from M3 BE to an external consumer. The Amazon S3 bucket is owned and maintained by the customer and is not a part of the hosted solution.

cards.question: Page 347 in 'Output Management' (Define Basic Data for Output Management) - detail 2: Explain 'To be able to write XML' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: To be able to write XML files to an S3 bucket, the Amazon S3 bucket location and credentials must be configured. This is performed in the Cloud File Transfer Agent Administration Tool in the tab Tenant Configuration. The Cloud File Transfer Agent Administration Tool is found in the M3 Administration Tools menu. • HTTPS Post The file is sent with HTTPS Post protocol. To be used with external third party output management solutions that support this communication type. • IDM Output Mgmt, double interface The file is sent to IDM Output Management using IDM’s Application Programming Interface (API), similar to communication type which is IDM Output Mgmt, single interface. The only difference is that communication type allows the ability to simultaneously process XML and CSV in the same batch job. Define available printers Use 'Printer.

cards.question: Page 347 in 'Output Management' (Define Basic Data for Output Management) - detail 3: Explain 'Open' (CRS290) to specify the printer' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Open' (CRS290) to specify the printer on which the document should be printed. The definition consists of an ID, description, name, printer address, warehouse, and printer group. Define ISO language code Use 'Language. Open' (CRS010) to define ISO language code The ISO language code is used when setting the field to 'local' in the XML file. Define media used for M3 standard business documents Use 'Document Media. Open' (CRS116) to define media and their relationship to the interface (output format). M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Output Management

cards.question: Page 348 in 'Output Management' (Define Basic Data for Output Management) - detail 1: Explain 'Generate all the standard media codes' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Generate all the standard media codes in M3 by function key F14='Standard'. Media categories define which output is delivered by the output server. Define M3 standard business documents Use 'M3 Document. Open' (CRS928) to define M3 standard business documents. A full set of documents is generated by function key F14='Standard'. You can also use the function key F15='Gen all'. This generates document media in (CRS116) and the connection between document media and standard documents in 'M3 Document. Connect Media' (CRS929). Note: 'M3 Document. Open' (CRS928) is a global program, so the settings affect all companies in M3. Document variants refer to the variants of document content structures.

cards.question: Page 348 in 'Output Management' (Define Basic Data for Output Management) - detail 2: Explain 'A dispatch advice document contains a' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: A dispatch advice document contains a specific content describing delivery. By using another document variant, you can restructure the content with details such as package, item, or order-oriented. Define media support for M3 business documents Use 'M3 Document. Connect Media' (CRS929) to define the media connected to documents. The definition states the media support per document number. By connecting a document number to a media code, that combination is validated. You can also generate the records in (CRS929) by selecting function key F15='Gen all' in 'M3 Document. Open' (CRS928). Define business documents for the installation Business documents are defined in 'Standard Document. Open' (CRS027). Generate all the business documents in M3 BE using the function key F14='Standard'.

cards.question: Page 348 in 'Output Management' (Define Basic Data for Output Management) - detail 3: Explain 'Generate all documents and then remove' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Generate all documents and then remove those that are not required. Define email body template from IDM Output Management Edit Email template in IDM Output Management and select the email design from M3. Choose Email template in 'Output Media Selection. Open' (MNS205/F) or 'Doc Media Control Object. Connect Media' (CRS949). Selection of email template Select Email template in (MNS205/F) or (CRS949/F). Email template is connected to 'Document Type. Open' (MNS060). M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Output Management

cards.question: Page 349 in 'Output Management' (Define Output Selection based on User) - detail 1: Explain 'From IDM Output Management, you can' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: From IDM Output Management, you can edit and upload your own email template connected to a specific document type that exists in (MNS060). When selecting an email template in (MNS205), this will search for a template that has been uploaded to IDM Output Management. Define Output Selection based on User This document explains how you define the output selection for available printer files based on the user who requests the output. Together with potentially thousands of users, the setup must be flexible enough to suit groups of users or printer files, and be specific enough to suit a single user and printer file combination.

cards.question: Page 349 in 'Output Management' (Define Output Selection based on User) - detail 2: Explain 'Workflow Use 'Output Service Selection. Open'' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Workflow Use 'Output Service Selection. Open' (MNS204) to define the output service connected to a printer file and user. This read logic is applied when running an Output function until a record is found in (MNS204): Printer fileUserDivisionSequence XXX01 XBlankX02 First positionsXX03 First positionsBlankX04 BlankXX05 BlankBlankX06 XXBlank07 XBlankBlank08 First positionsXBlank09 First positionsBlankBlank10 BlankXBlank11 BlankBlankBlank12 Hold output When testing a new output service, it is useful to hold the output and not distribute it to the output server. This is done by selecting the 'Hold output' field on (MNS204/E).

cards.question: Page 349 in 'Output Management' (Define Output Selection based on User) - detail 3: Explain 'The output is always distributed to' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: The output is always distributed to 'Output. Manage per Job' (MNS270). The 'Hold output' field is now activated for Infor Document Management (IDM) service IDs for both single and double interfaces. Confirm output M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Output Management

cards.question: Page 350 in 'Output Management' (Define Output Selection based on User) - detail 1: Explain 'Indicates if media settings are confirmed' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Indicates if media settings are confirmed before an Output job is started. The program used for confirmation is either (MNS212) or (MNS215) depending on parameter 'Program for confirmation of output job' in (CMS700). Retention policy for output files This selection field indicates the number of days that the output files are stored. With this new option, a retention policy is chosen based on the requirement for each printout. Priority queue for IDM Customer has an option in M3 to specify a separate queue that has a prioritization above the main print queue in IDM.

cards.question: Page 350 in 'Output Management' (Define Output Selection based on User) - detail 2: Explain 'This leads to improved print times' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: This leads to improved print times for the selected jobs. Select media In 'Output Media Selection. Open' (MNS205), the media information is connected to a printer file and user. The media information is then included in the output and used by an external Output Management application. In 'Settings – Output Management' (CMS700), the user can set where the field 'Location' in (MNS205) picks the value.

cards.question: Page 350 in 'Output Management' (Define Output Selection based on User) - detail 3: Explain 'If the check box 'Use 'User' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: If the check box 'Use 'User Location' in output media selection' in (CMS700) is selected, the user’s 'User location' field value in (MNS150) is used as the value of the field 'Location' in (MNS205) during the media output selection. If the check box in (CMS700) is not selected, the field 'Location' in (MNS205) is not considered, and the calling program is used as its value instead. Multiple media records can be set for the same printer file and user. This read logic is applied when running an output function until a record is found in (MNS205), and the check box in (CMS700) is not selected: UserPrinter fileDivisionSequence XXX01 BlankXX02 XFirst positionsX03 BlankFirst positionsX04 XBlankX05 BlankBlankX06 XXBlank07 BlankXBlank08 XFirst positionsBlank09 BlankFirst positionsBlank10 XBlankBlank11 BlankBlankBlank12 If the check box in (CMS700) is selected, this read logic is applied instead: M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Output Man

cards.question: Page 351 in 'Output Management' (Define Output Selection based on User) - detail 1: Explain 'LocationUserPrinter fileDivisionSequence XXXX01 BlankXXX02 XBlankXX03 BlankBlankXX04' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: LocationUserPrinter fileDivisionSequence XXXX01 BlankXXX02 XBlankXX03 BlankBlankXX04 XXFirst positionsX05 BlankXFirst positionsX06 XBlankFirst positionsX07 BlankBlankFirst positionsX08 XXBlankX09 BlankXBlankX10 XBlankBlankX11 BlankBlankBlankX12 BlankXXBlank13 BlankBlankXBlank14 BlankXFirst positionsBlank15 BlankBlankFirst positionsBlank16 BlankXBlankBlank17 BlankBlankBlankBlank18 Depending on the media that you have selected for the printer file, one of the E, F, G, or H panels is displayed. Connect printer file to printer (E panel) Specify the fields for the E panel: • Printer file • Default printer names • Whether field heading and contents are to be displayed • Overlay • Printer tray • Bin • Number of copies • Paper size • Whether to send the document to the archive.

cards.question: Page 351 in 'Output Management' (Define Output Selection based on User) - detail 2: Explain '• Interface - identify the output' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • Interface - identify the output to be created, either an XML or a CSV file. 'Field interface' is only applicable once the set value in (CMS005) is 1-'Override is allowed'.

cards.question: Page 351 in 'Output Management' (Define Output Selection based on User) - detail 3: Explain 'M3 Application Foundation User Guide |' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Output Management

cards.question: Page 352 in 'Output Management' (Define Output Selection based on User) - detail 1: Explain 'Connect printer file to email (F' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Connect printer file to email (F panel) Specify: • To email - indicates the recipient's email address. • From email - indicates the sender's email address. A validation is done to check if the sender's email address domain is correct. Infor delivers a standard configuration including a subset of fields based on the standard tables used in the print function (standard tables are tables which are always read by the output function). cards.question: Page 359 in 'Output Management' (Define XML File) - detail 1: Explain '• Root element: This field indicates' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • Root element: This field indicates the root element of an XML document. This must contain one root element that is the parent of a" other elements. The root element is the first and last tag in the XML document. • Default namespace – XML Namespace provides a method to avoid element name conflicts. In XML, the element names are defined by the developer. This often leads to conflicts when attempting to combine XML documents from different XML applications. The namespace is defined by an xmlns attribute in the start tag of an element. The namespace declaration has this syntax: xmlns:pre fix="URI". Defining a default namespace for an element saves us from using prefixes in all the child elements. Defining a default namespace has this syntax: xmlns="namespaceURI" The default namespace field must have a value for the other field, such as Namespace and Prefix for Namespace shown in the XML structure during printing. • Prefix for namespace 1:

cards.question: Page 359 in 'Output Management' (Define XML File) - detail 2: Explain 'The use of namespaces and prefixes' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: The use of namespaces and prefixes is recommended to remove any ambiguity for element and attribute declarations because several vocabularies are used to describe different elements and attributes in XML documents, the use of namespaces and prefixes is recommended to remove any ambiguity for element and attribute declarations. Distinguishing between element and attribute names for each namespace is essential when using schemas from more than one namespace. • Schema: This field indicates the XML schema. The XML schema describes the structure of XML documents. The purpose of an XML schema is to define the legal building blocks of an XML document. • Element name: This field indicates the element name that defines the name of the tag. cards.goodAnswer: Open Section' (CMS007/B), section type LINE_MAIN is by default set to status (Active), meaning it will be included in either the XML and CSV file. All other sections have status (Active for XML), which means it will only be included in the XML output. LINE_MAIN is the only section type available for Open Format. You can have multiple section types that are active for both XML and CSV. Use option 2='Change' to update the section status. • Table name The table name field only becomes visible once section status on 'XML Structure. Open Section' (CMS007/E) is updated to (Active for CSV) or (Active). The value is matched with a table name in the Excel template to locate where to insert the CSV data. • Sheet name M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Output Management

This field is matched with a sheet name in the Excel template to locate where to insert the CSV data. • Element name Can only be changed or deleted for financial report functionality. • Section attribute The F panel is used to display and define the attributes. A maximum of seven attributes per section can be defined. Attributes 1- depend on parameter 'Section attributes' and cannot be edited. • Attribute 1: Holds the value for Label • Attribute 2: Holds the value for Type • Attribute 3: Holds the value for Subtype. Attributes 4- are considered as custom attributes wherein you can specify or modify a label. A custom attribute is added on the XML structure of the section labels. Note: Duplicate attribute name is not allowed. If new predefined sections are delive

cards.question: Page 364 in 'Output Management' (Define XML File) - detail 2: Explain '• Extend section The purpose of' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • Extend section The purpose of this extend section field is to toggle on or off the extension point logic of the current section. When the field is selected, all field values are stored in a HashMap. Together with the other parameters such as printer file, batch job number, XML structure, variant, and section number, these are passed on to a batch program and made available in the XtendM3 script. • Grp by element This field indicates the ID of the element which groups the XML Section. A blank value indicates that the section is not grouped. A grouped section indicates that all XML sections printed sequentially are grouped together by the 'Grp by element' field. It also indicates that all element labels are moved to the grouping section. Leave this field blank for financial report functionality. • Label Labels are included on 'XML Structure. Open' (CMS007/E). These ways are used to decide how the label should be set: • Alternative - No

cards.question: Page 364 in 'Output Management' (Define XML File) - detail 3: Explain 'Translation is taken from Message file' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Translation is taken from Message file MVXCON. • Alternative - An overriding M3 BE message ID and message file can be specified. If no Message file is specified, MVXCON is used. • Alternative - An overriding text can be specified. The overriding text can be 1- characters long. Translation is specified using the language button. A check box 'Fallback to standard msg' is used when language set for the printer file is not specified by using the language button. Is the check box is activated, 'Standard message ID' is used as a label in the XML for a field, section, and variant. If the check box is not activated, the XML label of a field, section, or variant will have no label. M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Output Management

cards.question: Page 365 in 'Output Management' (Define XML File) - detail 2: Explain 'Open Section Elements' in (CMS007) to' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Open Section Elements' in (CMS007) to open (CMS009). The elements are the lowest level in the XML structure and define the content to be included in the XML file. The content is either data from a field in an M3 Business Engine table, a calculated field, or a manually specified value. The logic and functionality available in (CMS009) highly depends on the format selected in (CMS006). Note: Section type 'Grp Sec' does not allow creation of element in (CMS009). Common functionality used for all formats • Element type Elements with type (Text element) contain the data, while Elements with type and are used to create sub-sections (define start and end elements). • Source of data Used when text elements are the source of data specifying where the data should be retrieved from.

cards.question: Page 367 in 'Output Management' (Define XML File) - detail 2: Explain 'If the field 'Hide zero value'' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: If the field 'Hide zero value' is selected, then zero is included as blank, otherwise as 0,00. • Attributes (F panel) The F panel is used to define the Attributes for the elements. A maximum of seven attributes per element can be defined. Attributes and are reserved attributes and cannot be edited. • Attribute 1: Holds the value for the label (see above for details). • Attribute 2: Holds the value for Debit/Credit code (see above for details). • Field Output Settings (G panel) The G panel is used to define the field output settings for the elements. These settings apply only to text elements and affect the output as viewed from 'Output. Manage per Job' (MNS270) for the interfaces CSV, flat file, and XML. cards.question: Page 367 in 'Output Management' (Define XML File) - detail 3: Explain 'Alignment This field specifies the alignment' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Alignment This field specifies the alignment using these alternatives: • Alternative 0: No changes to the output. • Alternative 1: The field is left-aligned. • Alternative 2: The field is right-aligned. Display length This field specifies the length of the field as displayed in the output. This value overrides the actual field length. These are the conditions on the display length: • If the display length is shorter than the actual field length, truncation is based on the alignment value. • If the display length is longer than the actual field length, padding is based on the alignment value. M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Output Management

cards.question: Page 368 in 'Output Management' (Define XML File) - detail 1: Explain '• The minimum value is and' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • The minimum value is and the maximum is 9999. If the display length is set to 0, no changes is made to the output regardless of the alignment and padding character values. Padding char This field specifies the character used to pad the output. Padding is based on the alignment and display length values. These are the conditions on padding character: • If the display length is longer than the actual field length and the alignment is set to Left, padding characters is added to the right.

cards.question: Page 368 in 'Output Management' (Define XML File) - detail 2: Explain '• If the alignment is set' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • If the alignment is set to Right, padding characters is added to the left. • If the field is left blank, spaces is used for padding. Commas and double quotation marks cannot be used. Format - M3 standard document Element sequence number Used as the sequence of the element within a section in the XML file. The sequence number is divided into groups: CommentsElement sequence Reserved for predefined elements in the standard XML structures delivered (M3_STD_0x-0x).

cards.question: Page 368 in 'Output Management' (Define XML File) - detail 3: Explain 'You cannot add or delete elements' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: You cannot add or delete elements in this group 0001- Reserved for predefined elements in the standard XML structures delivered (M3_STD_0x-0x) where the table is unique for country configuration component. You cannot add or delete elements in this group 4001- Elements can be added, changed, or deleted7001- Element name If the source of data is set to 1-‘Field’ the element is set with these rules: • For database fields it is set equal to the 6-position database field name, for example, OAORNO or MMITNO. • For virtual fields is & replaced with VF, for example, VFCUCD or VFACAM • For other sources of data, the element name is set to ZZxxxx, where xxxx is the predefined suffix per source of data. XML example XML example with three elements <UIIVNO Label="Invoice no">201401561</UIIVNO> <VFORNO Label="CO no">1000025077</VFORNO> <OAWHLO Label="Warehouse">110</OAWHLO> M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Output Management

cards.question: Page 369 in 'Output Management' (Define XML File) - detail 1: Explain 'Format - M3 standard document For' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Format - M3 standard document For M3 standard report format, elements are structured in row and column format. Every column must belong to a row. In (CMS009), Start and end elements indicate a row, and all elements between those start and end elements belong to that row. • Element sequence number Used to sequence the element in the XML file and decide what row the element belongs to. A range of sequence numbers is reserved for every row: ReservedElement sequence number Reserved for Row 10002- Reserved for Row 21002- …… Reserved for Row 98002- • Add or delete rows Adding rows is done with function key F21='Add Row' which creates a start and end element for the next free row. Deleting rows is done with function key F22='Delete row' which deletes the start element, the end element, and all the text elements between, for the row with the highest number. • Column number Used to indicate the column number for the field in a layout. cards.goodAnswer: Column number is part of the element name. cards.question: Page 369 in 'Output Management' (Define XML File) - detail 3: Explain '• Disable field on job run' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • Disable field on job run level Specific fields may need to be disabled, so as not to be included in the XML file for the entire job. For example, the field 'VAT code' should only be included if VAT is used. This is controlled by the parameter Run disabled. The decision if a field should be disabled is based on a virtual field from section type REPORT. If the virtual field is true (value 1), the field is considered to be disabled. Alternatives for parameter Run disabled: • = No disable control • = Disable control - field is still included in XML file but value is set to blank if disabled • = Disable control and remove - field is removed from XML file if disabled M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Output Management

The API program and transaction to be run are specified in the XML file. During import, decide if the existing data should be replaced or not. If data should be replaced, a Delete transaction is run before the Add transaction. Checking import logs An import log is generated once Log to file on the Import Configuration Data window is selected. An option Download logs is displayed once import is successfully executed. Import log displays the configurations, such as transaction name, table name, field name and information describing a certain transaction that is not imported. Note: Import log must be checked if there is a discrepancy between the source environment or company, and the destination environment or company. The log gives information about what could be missing or incorrect configur

cards.question: Page 382 in 'Output Management' (Define XML File) - detail 2: Explain 'Details about configuration data XML file' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Details about configuration data XML file The content part of the configuration data file always includes these sections: Check, Delete, and Add. It is processed from beginning to end. • Check Optional section. Specification of API transactions that must be checked for related data before starting the processing of delete and add transactions. If error message is received from the API transactions, the process ends, and the add and delete sections are not processed. • Delete Required section. Specification of API transactions requiring deletion of existing data. The Delete section is only processed if check box 'Replace existing data' is selected in the tool wizard. There is usually one API transaction specified, but if required, several API transactions are listed. Error or warning messages from API transactions are logged but XML continues to process the next API transaction.

cards.question: Page 382 in 'Output Management' (Define XML File) - detail 3: Explain '• Add Required section Specification of' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • Add Required section Specification of API transactions requiring additional data. The Add section is always processed if no errors are found during the Check section. Usually, one API transaction is specified, but if required, several API transactions are listed. Error or warning messages from API transactions are logged but XML continues to process the next API transaction. cards.question: Page 384 in 'Output Management' (Design and Structure of XML File) - detail 2: Explain 'Design and Structure of XML File' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Design and Structure of XML File This document explains the design and structure of the XML file. Two different standards are available depending on the format selected in 'XML Structure. Open' (CMS006). Common standards are used for all formats. Data type Decimal Data with Data type Decimal are included in the XML according to these rules: • Without thousand separator • Decimal format, always period (.) • Rounded to correct number of decimals and adjusted per price quantity Example: 9999999. Metadata for how a decimal field should be formatted is included in the section Localization.

Debit/Credit code Whether Debit/Credit code should be used is indicated in Element DebitCreditCodeUsed (true/false), section DocumentInformation. <DocumentInformation> <DebitCreditCodeUsed>true</DebitCreditCodeUsed> Currency amount fields that should use Debit/Credit code has an additional attribute "D-C" including the Debit/Credit code sign. <VFORTO D-C="D">68325.00</VFORTO> <VFTOPY D-C="D">68325.00</VFTOPY> Data type date Data with data type date are included in this format: YYYY-MM-DD. cards.question: Page 385 in 'Output Management' (Design and Structure of XML File) - detail 1: Explain '• [YYYY] indicates a four-digit year' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • [YYYY] indicates a four-digit year, [MM] indicates a two-digit month, [DD] indicates a two-digit day. • Each date value has a fixed number of digits that is padded with leading zeros. • The separator used between date values is the hyphen (-). XML example without relation to time zone: <UHIVDT>2016-03-21</UHIVDT> XML example with relation to time zone: If the date has a relation to a time zone is the offset from the UTC time added as positive or negative value behind the date. There is no need to use the UTC time offset when using the date on a document, it is only used if the date need to be converted to UTC time.

cards.question: Page 385 in 'Output Management' (Design and Structure of XML File) - detail 2: Explain '<UHIVDT>2016-03-21+5:00</UHIVDT> Metadata for how a date' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: <UHIVDT>2016-03-21+5:00</UHIVDT> Metadata for how a date field should be formatted is included in section localization: <DateFormat>YYMMDD</DateFormat> <DateEditing>/<DateEditing> <Locale>en-GB<Locale> We also recommend that you use the locale settings for formatting of dates. Data type time Data with data type time are included in the format hh:mm:ss • [hh] indicates the hour, [mm] indicates the minutes, [ss] indicates the seconds. • Each time value has a fixed number of digits that is padded with leading zeros. • The separator used between date values is (:). XML example without relation to time zone: 14:05: <UHTIID>14:05:23</UHTIID> XML example with relation to time zone: If the date has a relation to a time zone is the offset from the UTC time added as positive or negative value behind the time.

cards.goodAnswer: • - FF02 - Free field • - FF03 - Free field • - FF04 - Free field • - FF05 - Free field • - FF06 - Free field • - FF07 - Free field • - FF08 - Free field • - FF09 - Free field • - FF010 - Free field • - RGS655PF Report Selected attribute value (60-69) on (MNS060/E) should correspond to the free fields on (CMS006/H). Monitoring Output Jobs This document explains how you track output jobs and confirm that delivery to the correct output Service ID. You can also monitor and manage output errors. 'Output. Manage per Job' (MNS270) and 'Output. Manage per Partner' (MNS207) are used to track and monitor output. Note that the XML file is only saved in (MNS270) and (MNS207) if 'Save output' in (MNS204) is selected.

cards.question: Page 395 in 'Output Management' (Monitoring Output Jobs) - detail 2: Explain 'Otherwise, it is automatically removed when' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Otherwise, it is automatically removed when the job is successfully completed. For every output function job, a record is created in (MNS270) that acts as the control record for the output. Every job is identified by a unique job number. This record contains the process status, process control, any messages received from the output server, and user IDs (submit external user ID and status external user ID) used to connect to Infor Document Management (IDM). The job number is connected to one or several output files (physical files) that have the Job number as the name. Object controlled output If an output function job uses output selection based on content, one or several additional records can be created in (MNS207). Records in (MNS270) containing output jobs that have (MNS207) records are marked with an exclamation mark (!). Use option 13='Partner' in (MNS270) to start (MNS207).

cards.question: Page 395 in 'Output Management' (Monitoring Output Jobs) - detail 3: Explain 'Example: The output function selects five' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Example: The output function selects five purchase orders and is directed to a printer. Two of these orders have object controlled output selection defined for media *MAIL. When the output job is running, three purchase orders go to the (MNS270) output file and two purchase orders create two addition records in (MNS207). Output status An output job can have different statuses depending on the communication type used on 'Output Service. Open' (MNS216/E). These statuses are used for Communication type IDM Output Management: M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Output Management

cards.question: Page 396 in 'Output Management' (Maintaining Output Jobs) - detail 1: Explain '• OPN = Open - the' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • OPN = Open - the job is running and XML file is being created. • PND = Pending - the XML file has been created and is waiting to be sent. • HLD = Hold - the XML file is put on hold. • SND = Sending - the XML file is being sent. • MSG = Sending error - an error has occurred when sending the XML file. • WRK = Process output - the XML file has been sent and output is being processed and distributed. • FAI = Output error- an error has occurred during processing or distribution of output. • FIN = Output completed – output has been completed and has been distributed successfully. These statuses are used for Communication type TCP/IP connector, Infor Reporting event and Transfer files: • OPN = Open - the job is running and file is being created. • PND = Pending - the file has been created and is waiting to be sent. • HLD = Hold - the file is hold.

cards.question: Page 396 in 'Output Management' (Maintaining Output Jobs) - detail 2: Explain '• SND = Sending - the' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • SND = Sending - the file is being sent. • MSG = Sending error - an error has occurred when sending the file. • SAV = Sent & saved - the file has been sent and has been saved. View output file Select the record of the job number, then select related option 11='View'. The M3 Business Engine Browse window is displayed. Select the required job number, and click 'Select'. A new tab with the output file is displayed. You can use the options 'Download' or 'Show XML'. Select the record of the job number and select related option 16='View JSON file'. The M3 BE Browse window is displayed. JSON file is used when doing the resend functionality for service IDs under Infor Document Management (IDM) only.

cards.question: Page 396 in 'Output Management' (Maintaining Output Jobs) - detail 3: Explain 'Note: Ensure that the pop-up blocker' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Note: Ensure that the pop-up blocker is disabled on the browser. If not, move over your pointer to the address bar, then click the Pop-up blocked icon. If a message is displayed, select the first option for allowing pop-ups. Send output file If 'Hold output' in (MNS204) is selected, the output file goes to either (MNS270) or (MNS207), but is not sent until it is manually sent by option 1='Send'. IDM Output Management If Communication type IDM Output Management is used, only related option 12='Output. Manage per file' can be used to see the detailed status for every XML file sent to IDM. In 'Output. Manage per file' (MNS209), the same statuses in (MNS270) are used. Maintaining Output Jobs This document explains how you manage and maintain output jobs. The process includes resending output manually and automatically, as well as deleting batches of output and obsolete output. M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Output Management

cards.question: Page 397 in 'Output Management' (Maintaining Output Jobs) - detail 1: Explain 'Workflow • Manual resending of output' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Workflow • Manual resending of output files Output files can be resent in 'Output. Manage per Job' (MNS270) by selecting option 1='Send'. For Streamserve and storyteller service ID, you can also change the service ID on (MNS270/E) before resending the file. For IDM service ID (single interface and double interface), changing of service ID is not allowed. (MNS270/E) is disabled. • Automatic resending of output files If, for some reason, the output cannot be sent, you can automatically resend output files by setting up the MNS950 Auto job in 'Subsystem Job. Open' (MNS050). In each run, the MNS950 job tries to resend each output file with status 'MSG' in (MNS270) for a maximum of three days. Message ID S_02012 in MNS950 is used for processing Infor Document Management (IDM) connection failures (that is, IDM server is down or not responding).

cards.question: Page 397 in 'Output Management' (Maintaining Output Jobs) - detail 2: Explain 'When using an IDM service ID' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: When using an IDM service ID (communication type and 6), the automatic resending of output will only be triggered when the (MNS270) job status is MSG, and when the error message ID is S_02012. The number of retries or the time limit set for Streamserve is also applied to IDM, so that only records created in the last hours are resent. For grouped output, when manually resending records in 'Output. Manage per Grouped Content' (MNS271), the (MNS270) status stays at MSG until all records in (MNS271) have been resent successfully. This means when MNS950 runs, it will still resend the (MNS271) records that have been manually resent. For grouped and ungrouped outputs mixed in the same (MNS270) job, manually resending an ungrouped output first changes that message ID to S_01853. When MNS950 runs, it will not be able to resend it because message ID is not S_02012. • You can only resend the Output files if the XML exists. You must enable the 'Sav

cards.question: Page 397 in 'Output Management' (Maintaining Output Jobs) - detail 3: Explain 'Open' (MNS204). The XML availability depends' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Open' (MNS204). The XML availability depends on the retention policy of M3 using the 'Ret policy file' field. You can set the field from three to days. • Manual deletion of output files You can delete output files manually in (MNS270). By selecting 'Delete', you remove the control record, the output file, and the related records and files. • Delete obsolete files in batch You can delete obsolete output files in batch through 'Output Data. Delete' (MNS906). The delete function can be run on a regular basis if (MNS906) is defined in the M3 scheduler. M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Output Management

cards.question: Page 398 in 'Ad Hoc Reporting' (Connect Ad Hoc Reports to Users and Roles) - detail 1: Explain 'Chapter 7: Ad Hoc Reporting Connect' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Chapter 7: Ad Hoc Reporting Connect Ad Hoc Reports to Users and Roles This document describes how to connect users and roles to ad hoc reports. Background Ad hoc reports can be connected to specific users and users connected with roles. The reason for the two ways to connect users is that if there are many users, it is easier and faster to connect the report to a role, which has multiple users connected to it. The program 'Ad Hoc Report by User or Role. Connect' (AHS120) is used to connect ad hoc reports to users and roles. However, a report record cannot connect to both a user and a role. If a report must be connected to both a user and a role, two records are created in (AHS120) – one with user and one with role. In the program 'Ad Hoc Report by User. Open' (AHS200), each user can see which reports they are connected to. The field 'Generated by role' in (AHS200) identifies which records in (AHS200) have been generated by roles and whi

Doc loc path Define Ad Hoc Report With M3 Ad Hoc Reporting tool, M3 super users and IT support personnel can create and maintain flexible user-defined report layouts based on the M3 Business Engine database. With the tool, you can generate reports in an XML format to be consumed by products such as Streamserve or Excel. These are the advantages for using the tool: • Enabling the creation of operational reports and listings direct from the M3 BE application database • Increasing business agility and speed by delivering ad hoc reports on demand • Automatically knowing M3 and respecting user security privileges. In the M3 Ad Hoc report designer, you can design

cards.question: Page 400 in 'Ad Hoc Reporting' (Define Ad Hoc Report) - detail 2: Explain 'These alternatives are valid for the' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: These alternatives are valid for the 'Design tool': • Alternative 0: When selected, 'Information Browser. Open' (CMS100) is used to configure an ad hoc report. You can design custom lists by adding virtual fields and preview the result before you generate the report. • Alternative 1: When selected, 'Ad Hoc Report Designer. Open' (AHS112) is used. In this program, options to open the configurable XML programs are available to configure an ad hoc report. This connects the ad hoc report to the configurable XML programs. Other configurable XML functionalities, such as custom sections, custom subtotal sections, and global sequence number can then be used to configure an ad hoc report. The limitation of positions and columns is also removed.

cards.question: Page 400 in 'Ad Hoc Reporting' (Define Ad Hoc Report) - detail 3: Explain 'For every ad hoc report group' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.

When creating a new report, a report text is required. M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Ad Hoc Reporting
