cards.question: Page 403 in 'Display Management' (Creating Bookmarks) - detail 1: Explain 'Chapter 8: Display Management Bookmarks in' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Chapter 8: Display Management Bookmarks in M3 BE This document provides an overview of the bookmark functionality in M3 BE. Overview With a bookmark, you can start an interactive Business Engine program with: • A specific detail panel and for a specific record. • A specific list panel positioned on a specific record. When starting a detail panel, you can also position the cursor at a specific field. For example, you can start 'Item. Connect Warehouse' (MMS002) directly on panel F for a specific item in a specific warehouse.

cards.question: Page 403 in 'Display Management' (Creating Bookmarks) - detail 2: Explain 'A bookmark can be specified to' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: A bookmark can be specified to perform any option available in the interactive program on the record. The bookmark specifies whether the start panel is included or excluded, and which start panel to use. The panel sequence, sorting order, view, and field values on the start panel can also be set for the bookmark. Bookmarks are used in these scenarios: • H5 – A bookmark can be saved as a start page shortcut. • Mashup - Mashups use bookmarks to display a BE detail or list panel. • Enterprise search – When a record in a table is found in Enterprise search, bookmarks are used to start the maintenance program for the record.

cards.question: Page 403 in 'Display Management' (Creating Bookmarks) - detail 3: Explain 'For an interactive program to be' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: For an interactive program to be able to process bookmarks, a specific program code is required. The bookmark code for M3 BE is either delivered in base delivery or in a code template. This means that not all programs are enabled for bookmarks. Creating Bookmarks This document explains how you create bookmarks in H5 and how to test bookmark parameters. Outcome A bookmark is created in H5 which lets you open a specific Business Engine program. M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Display Management

cards.question: Page 404 in 'Display Management' (Creating Bookmarks) - detail 1: Explain 'Create a bookmark in H5 Create' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Create a bookmark in H5 Create a bookmark using M3 Business Engine in the H5 client: Launch the M3 BE program you want to bookmark. For example, open 'Item. Open' (MMS001). Click the Infor M3 tab arrow and select Bookmark this page. Note: The bookmark is saved under the Bookmarks section in the Navigation menu pane. Add the bookmark widget Follow these steps to add the shortcut widget on your Start Page in the H5 client: On the Start Pages, click 'Add Widget'.

cards.goodAnswer: Select the 'Shortcuts' widget. Bookmark parameters Developers can use (MTS043/A) to test how the bookmark parameters settings change the BE program behavior, for example, when working with Mashups. Specify the required field values as described in the table with parameters to set and click Next to test calling on the program using the bookmark settings. A bookmark contains several parameter values. The parameter values decide how the BE program will behave when it is started from a bookmark. This table shows how to set these parameters: Value (example)DescriptionParameter MMS002The BE program to start, does not need to be a program in the menu.

Open' (CMS010) Parameters to set Label The label for a specific field is controlled by the 'Label method' field using these methods: • = No label The field has no label attribute. • = Standard message ID (default value) The standard M3 message ID is retrieved from the file where the field originates. The label text is retrieved from the message file MVXCON in the end user's M3 language. • = Overriding message ID An overriding M3 message ID and a message file are specified. The label text is retrieved from the specified message file in the end user's M3 language. If no message file is specified, MVXCON is used. • = Overriding name An overriding label text is specified.

Manage Sorting Orders In this document the creation of user-defined sorting orders in different programs is described. The sorting order determines the sorting order of the fields and the search capabilities displayed on the panel. Outcome A user-defined sorting order will be created. One or several views will have been connected to the sorting order. M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Display Management

cards.question: Page 407 in 'Display Management' (Manage Sorting Orders) - detail 1: Explain 'Sorting orders are stored in the' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Sorting orders are stored in the CSYVIU table. Sorting orders are connected to sorting options, views and selection tables. Using sorting orders enables you to display only the piece of information that you will need. Before you start A view must have been created. See Manage Views on page 411. Follow these steps When creating a sorting order, you can choose whether you want to start using it in a program or directly in 'Sorting Order. Open' (CRS022). In some programs, you have the possibility of creating selection tables in 'General Selection Table. Open' (CRS023) and 'Selection Table. Select Fields' (CRS026). To create a sorting order, open the 'Sorting order. Open' (CRS022/B) panel. Another way of launching (CRS022) is to press F4 twice in the 'Sorting order' field of the program in which you are working, such as 'Delivery. Open Toolbox' (MWS410). If you have started on the (CRS022/B) panel, fill out the 'Program' field and press Ent

cards.question: Page 407 in 'Display Management' (Manage Sorting Orders) - detail 2: Explain 'Define a sorting order in the' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Define a sorting order in the 'Sorting order' field. Press Enter. Specify values in the 'Description' and 'Name' fields on the (CRS022/E) panel. In the 'Sorting Option' field on the (CRS022/E) panel, press F4 twice to open the 'Sorting Option. Open' (CRS021/B) panel. To create a user-defined sorting order, you must create (or select) sorting options in (CRS021). In addition, select the number of filters in (CRS022) and the views in 'View. Open' (CRS020). To create a sorting option, go to step 9. On the (CRS022/E) panel, fill out the 'Number of filters' field. The field indicates the number of key fields that will act as filters in the selected sorting option. The fields are selected starting from the first field and then field by field throughout the key definition. Note: A value must be entered in fields that work as filters if the record should be displayed. In the 'View' field on the E panel, select the desired views for your sorting

cards.question: Page 407 in 'Display Management' (Manage Sorting Orders) - detail 3: Explain 'Open' (CRS020/B) panel. Select your desired' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Open' (CRS020/B) panel. Select your desired alternatives and then press Enter. Pressing Enter opens the (CRS022/E) panel and your selected view will be displayed. Continue to the next view field to have several views from which to choose. You may select up to six views for your sorting order. If you want to create a view of your own, see Manage Views on page 411. To create a sorting option, click in the 'Sorting Option' field on the (CRS022/E) panel and press F4 twice to open the 'Sorting Option. Open' (CRS021/B) panel. On the (CRS021/B) panel, define a sorting option in the 'Sorting option' field and then press Enter. Specify values in the 'Description' and 'Name' fields on the (CRS021/E) panel. In the 'Key' field, press F4 twice. On the 'Field Group. Display Permitted Fields' (CRS109/B) panel, select the fields that you want as sorting fields. cards.question: Page 408 in 'Display Management' (Manage Sorting Orders) - detail 1: Explain 'On the E panel, there could' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.

The 'Descending' field controls whether or not sorting of the field values should be descending. The 'Aggregation' field indicates whether to aggregate the records in the list view. The valid alternatives are: = No. Display detailed. = Yes. Aggregate if the information in the selected fields matches. Note that times and quantities are totaled during aggregation. In the 'Field' column on the (CRS021/F) panel, define which database fields to use. Press F4 twice and choose among the fields on the 'Field Group.

cards.question: Page 409 in 'Display Management' (Manage Sorting Orders) - detail 2: Explain 'View 1-6(CRS022/F) If sorting option is' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: View 1-6(CRS022/F) If sorting option is used (SOUS = 2), additional parameters are shown. • Change filter This defines whether the number of filters should be changeable in the very program. • Max number of records The maximum number of database records (database reads) that will be performed. If the maximum number is reached, the sub field build is stopped and an error message is displayed. The field is used to ensure that the computer has the capacity to manage a large number of records. If no value is entered in this field, the number of records will be maximized to 1,000. Normally, the value is greater than 1, and less than 10,000. 'Records' refer to the number of items, customers, etc. • Single division This controls whether the sorting order should only display records for a single division.

cards.question: Page 409 in 'Display Management' (Manage Sorting Orders) - detail 3: Explain 'If this field is selected, 'Division'' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: If this field is selected, 'Division' must be selected as the first filter. Users that are working in a single division (local users) can only use sorting orders that have the 'Single division' field selected. Users that are working in all divisions (central users) can use all sorting orders regardless of the setting in the 'Single division' field. • Selection field 1- The ID of the three fields that should be used as 'From' and 'To' selection fields in the program. • Aggregation Aggregation is used to accumulate several records into one record. Aggregation levels are used to indicate how records in the list will be aggregated. Key fields that are not included in the aggregation level will be aggregated. The presentation of aggregated fields is defined per view and field in the aggregation M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Display Management

cards.question: Page 410 in 'Display Management' (Manage Sorting Options) - detail 1: Explain 'display rule. The number of aggregated' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: display rule. The number of aggregated records is always stored in the S0NAGG field. This field can be used to find out if a subfile record is aggregated. The virtual field &NAGG is automatically added to the field group and can be used for the view to display the number of aggregated lines. • Change aggregation This controls whether aggregation should be changeable in the very program. • Subtotal 1- Subtotals are used to insert a subtotal record when the value in a specific key field is updated. Any key field in the selected sorting option can be used as a break level for the subtotal. The presentation of the subtotal record is defined per view and field in the subtotal rule. • Translate description For programs supporting configurable views, the new check box 'Translate description' has been added on 'Sorting Order. Open' (CRS022/E). If the check box is selected, a sorting order description is generated automatically per language in (

In some programs, you have the possibility to create selection tables in 'General Selection Table. Open' (CRS023) and 'Selection Table. Select Fields' (CRS026). Manage Sorting Options When you create sorting options, you can either select or delete a certain field value after the sorting order is displayed. This document describes how to create a user-defined sorting option in 'M3 File. Create Sorting Options' (CRS021), which can be opened directly or accessed by pressing F4 from the 'Sorting Option' field on the 'Sorting Order. Open' (CRS022/E) panel. User-defined sorting options can only have these values: U1–U9, V1–V9, or X1–X9. Follow these steps On the (CRS021/B) panel, define a sorting

cards.question: Page 410 in 'Display Management' (Manage Sorting Options) - detail 3: Explain 'Specify values in the description and' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Specify values in the description and name fields on (CRS021/E). In the key field, press F4. Select the fields that you want as sorting fields. On the (CRS021/E) panel, descending fields may be enabled, depending on which file you make sorting options for. This functionality controls whether or not sorting of the field values should be descending. In the 'Field' column on (CRS021/F), define which database fields to use. Press F4 and choose from the database fields. In the Value field, specify the values to test and 'Select/Omit' shows whether these values should be included or excluded. By limiting the fields and values, the record will be displayed faster than if no selection is made. Raise the status of a recently created sorting option by choosing alternative 20='Activate' on the (CRS021/B) panel. Press F5= 'Refresh' to display the new status. M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Display Management

cards.question: Page 411 in 'Display Management' (Manage Views) - detail 1: Explain 'By exporting the configuration, you can' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: By exporting the configuration, you can save or transfer the sorting options. Use alternative 22='Export Configuration' on one or several sorting options and specify the file name and version number of the configuration. Locate the file by opening 'Configuration Data Jobs. Open' (MNS200). Use sorting order 4, then specify the file name and start date. Use alternative 20='Display XML file' to download the XML file as a compressed zip file. After import through the Business Engine Configuration Data tool, the sorting options are activated automatically. Sorting options can also be accessed and managed through the API CRS021MI (Sorting option interface). Manage Views This document describes the creation of user-defined views in different programs. The views control which information or field to display on a panel. Outcome A user-defined view is created.

cards.question: Page 411 in 'Display Management' (Manage Views) - detail 2: Explain 'The view helps display only the' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: The view helps display only the piece of information that a user requires. The information is stored in these files: • View (CSYSTP) • Key (CSYSTR) Before you start You must generate these items: • A field group in 'Field Group. Open' (CRS108) • Fields in 'Field Group. Display Permitted Fields' (CRS109) Follow these steps To create a view, open the 'View. Open' (CRS020/B) panel. Specify the 'Program' field on (CRS020/B). The field indicates the program where the view is used. Create a name for your view in the 'View' field. Depending on the value of the responsible active, (CRS020/E) or 'View. Select Columns' (CRS016/B) is displayed. Open the (CRS020/E) panel and fill out the 'Field' column by pressing F4 and then selecting among the database fields on 'Field Group.

cards.question: Page 411 in 'Display Management' (Manage Views) - detail 3: Explain 'Display Permitted Fields' (CRS109/B). The database' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Display Permitted Fields' (CRS109/B). The database field selection is dependent on the given program. Specify the 'Field sequence number' on (CRS020/E). You may change the order in which the database fields must be displayed. To change the order, switch the numbers. In the 'Field length' column, optionally specify a value to shorten unnecessarily long fields in M3. If no value is specified, the field length follows the M3 standard. Open the F panel and specify values in the 'Description' and 'Name' fields. When you press Enter, your new view is displayed on (CRS020/B). M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Display Management

cards.question: Page 412 in 'Display Management' (Manage Views) - detail 1: Explain 'Additional fields on the (CRS020/E) panel' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Additional fields on the (CRS020/E) panel Depending on which program the view is connected to, the fields displayed on (CRS020/E) may vary. In some programs, you can find these fields on the E panel: • The 'Sorting sequence' field indicates in which order the record must be sorted in a certain view. The sorting order given here overrules the sorting order defined as the sorting option in use. • In the 'Maximum value' field, specify the cumulated maximum value for the record. All values beyond this limit are highlighted when displayed. • If you are creating a view for the program 'Delivery. Open Toolbox' (MWS410), the 'Override max value' field is displayed. This is where you indicate whether the maximum value specified in the view is replaced by the corresponding value for the current shipment, when present. Select among these values: • = No, do not replace. • = Yes, replace with the gross capacity of the shipment, if present. This alte

cards.question: Page 412 in 'Display Management' (Manage Views) - detail 2: Explain 'This alternative is relevant when comparing' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: This alternative is relevant when comparing a collection of planned deliveries to what is left of capacity in a shipment. Note: This option only applies to columns that contain information relating to the shipment capacity units: weight, volume, and the freely defined unit. Translate description on the (CRS016/B) panel For views that are maintained in (CRS016), there is a check box 'Translate description' (ATRA), and a Language button. If the check box is selected, a view description is generated automatically per language in 'Language Handling. Open' (CRS830) the first time this is required. An automatically generated description is based on the sorting order. For example, a view connected to sorting order is given the description 'Standard view for sorting order 1' If the 'Translate description' check box is not selected, a description must be maintained manually in (CRS016) or per language in (CRS830). The languages to be used must b

cards.question: Page 412 in 'Display Management' (Manage Views) - detail 3: Explain 'Open' (CMS010), 'Ad Hoc Report Group' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Open' (CMS010), 'Ad Hoc Report Group. Open' (AHS100) and 'Table Browser. Open' (MNS175) are supported. It is also triggered from (CRS020) and (CRS016). Parameters to set The field indicates …FieldProgram ID / Panel the program in which the view is used. Refer to the field help for valid programs. Program(CRS020/B) the view that is in use. Views are user-defined and determines the sorting order and the col- umn layout. View(CRS020/B) the information from the file to be displayed. Those fields are also used to create the contents of user-defined files. Field(CRS020/E) M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Display Management

cards.question: Page 413 in 'Display Management' (Sorting Options: Select/Omit) - detail 1: Explain 'The field indicates …FieldProgram ID /' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: The field indicates …FieldProgram ID / Panel the field length, which also determines the column width. If the field is blank, the field length follows the M3 standard. Field length(CRS020/E) the order in which each key field is displayed.Field sequence numbers(CRS020/E) how the records in the view must be sorted. The information given in this field overrules the sorting order of the sorting option in use. Sorting sequence(CRS020/E) the cumulated maximum value for the records that must be displayed without being highlight- ed. Maximum value(CRS020/E) the value that must be valid instead of the maximum value.

cards.question: Page 413 in 'Display Management' (Sorting Options: Select/Omit) - detail 2: Explain 'This parameter is only relevant when' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: This parameter is only relevant when browsing deliveries that are not connect- ed to a shipment. Override max value(CRS020/E) the number of decimal places displayed in the numerical fields containing decimals. You may specify to decimals. If nothing is speci- fied, the number of decimal places defined in the database is displayed. Note: Fields for price, amount, and quantity are often regulated by settings in the system. These settings override the number specified here.

cards.question: Page 413 in 'Display Management' (Sorting Options: Select/Omit) - detail 3: Explain 'Number of decimal places(CRS020/E) an edit' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Number of decimal places(CRS020/E) an edit code. You can select a predefined edit code to manage how numeric fields should be displayed, for example whether decimal point or comma must be used. Edit code(CRS020/E) Sorting Options: Select/Omit When you create sorting options, you can choose whether a certain field value should be selected or omitted when the sorting order is displayed. This document describes the rules, valid combinations, and invalid combinations for the ‘Select/Omit’ field on the (CRS021/F) panel. Outcome Combinations for the Select/Omit and Operator fields are described. M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Display Management

cards.question: Page 414 in 'Display Management' (Sorting Options: Select/Omit) - detail 1: Explain 'Sorting options are stored in the' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Sorting options are stored in the CSYSOR table. Sorting Options: Select/Omit allows you to display records faster when you make selections than when no selections are made. Also, it allows easier search options for relevant information. Description Valid alternatives for Select/Omit The valid alternatives for the Select/Omit field are: • O = Omit these fields and their values to display • S = Select these fields and their values to display • Blank = This record will have the same value as the record above (O or S) Note: It is strongly recommended not to use O = Omit.

cards.question: Page 414 in 'Display Management' (Sorting Options: Select/Omit) - detail 2: Explain 'Valid alternatives for operator The alternatives' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Valid alternatives for operator The alternatives are: • EQ = Equal to • NE = Not equal to • LT = Less than • LE = Less than or equal to • GT = Greater than • GE = Greater than or equal to. Note: Avoid using alternative NE. Rules when S (Select) is used If you use only Select (S), then selected records and their selected values will be displayed. No other records and values will be displayed.

cards.question: Page 414 in 'Display Management' (Sorting Options: Select/Omit) - detail 3: Explain 'Note: Only operator EQ is used' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Note: Only operator EQ is used in these examples. • One select ValueOpFieldS/O 1000EQCustomer noOACUNOS Result: Only records that contain customer are displayed. • Repetitive select ValueOpFieldS/O 1000EQCustomer noOACUNOS 1001EQCustomer noOACUNOS Result: M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Display Management

cards.question: Page 415 in 'Display Management' (Sorting Options: Select/Omit) - detail 1: Explain 'Only records that contain customer and' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Only records that contain customer and are displayed Note: Avoid using different fields when you have repetitive S. Technically it works, but it can be difficult to logically understand the results. • Select and blank ValueOpFieldS/O 1000EQCustomer noOACUNOS FFOEQCO typeOAORTP Result: Records that contain customer with order type FFO are displayed. Blank is the same as ‘and’, referring to the row above.

cards.question: Page 415 in 'Display Management' (Sorting Options: Select/Omit) - detail 2: Explain '• Repetitive select and blank ValueOpFieldS/O' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • Repetitive select and blank ValueOpFieldS/O 1000EQcustomer numberOACUNOS FFOEQcustomer orderOAORTP 1001EQcustomer numberOACUNOS FFOEQcustomer orderOAORTP Result: Records that contain customer with order type FFO, and customer with order type FFO, are selected. Rules when O (Omit) is used If you use only Omit (O), then omitted records and their omitted values will not be displayed. All other records and values will be displayed. It is strongly recommended not to use O = Omit.

cards.question: Page 415 in 'Display Management' (Sorting Options: Select/Omit) - detail 3: Explain 'Note: Only operator EQ is used' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Note: Only operator EQ is used in these examples. • One omit ValueOpFieldS/O 1000EQCustomer noOACUNOO Result: Records that do not contain customer are selected. • Repetitive omit M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Display Management

cards.question: Page 416 in 'Display Management' (Sorting Options: Select/Omit) - detail 1: Explain 'ValueOpFieldS/O 1000EQCustomer noOACUNOO 1001EQCustomer noOACUNOO Result' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: ValueOpFieldS/O 1000EQCustomer noOACUNOO 1001EQCustomer noOACUNOO Result: Records that do not contain customer and are selected. Note: Avoid using different fields when you have repetitive O. Technically it works, but it can be difficult to logically understand the results. • Omit and blank You are not allowed to have blanks after O.

cards.question: Page 416 in 'Display Management' (Sorting Options: Select/Omit) - detail 2: Explain 'M3 will ignore blanks after O' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: M3 will ignore blanks after O. Rules when Select and Omit are used Basic rules • You cannot have O after S. • You cannot have O after S. Note: Only operator EQ is used in these examples.

cards.question: Page 416 in 'Display Management' (Sorting Options: Select/Omit) - detail 3: Explain '• One O, One S ValueOpFieldS/O' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • One O, One S ValueOpFieldS/O 1000EQcustomer numberOACUNOO FFOEQcustomer orderOAORTPS Result: Except for customer 1000, records that contain order type FFO are selected. • Repetitive O, One S ValueOpFieldS/O 1000EQcustomer numberOACUNOO 1001EQcustomer numberOACUNOO FFOEQcustomer orderOAORTPO Result: Except for customer and 1001, records that contain order type FFO are selected. M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Display Management

cards.question: Page 417 in 'Display Management' (Create Selection Tables) - detail 1: Explain 'Create Selection Tables This document explains' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Create Selection Tables This document explains how to create user-defined selection tables in different programs. A selection table makes it possible to select the information displayed on the panel. Outcome A user-defined selection table connected to a sorting order will have been created. With a selection table connected to your sorting order, you will limit the information displayed on the panel even more, and in that way exclude data that is irrelevant for your purposes. Before you start A view and a sorting order must have been created. Refer to documents in See Also section. Follow these steps You can create sorting order from any program or directly in 'Sorting order. Open' (CRS022). In order to create a user-defined sorting order, you need to create (or select) sorting options in 'Sorting Options' (CRS021). In addition, you select the number of filters in (CRS022) and the views in 'View. Open' (CRS020). In some programs, you hav

cards.question: Page 417 in 'Display Management' (Create Selection Tables) - detail 2: Explain 'Open' (CRS023) and 'Selection Table. Define' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Open' (CRS023) and 'Selection Table. Define Fields (CRS026). Create Selection Tables In some programs, you may create selection tables from the (CRS022/F) panel. A selection table makes it possible to choose from and to values in different fields and in that way select the information displayed on the panel. The programs where you can create selection tables are the following: • 'Item. Open Toolbox' (MMS200) • 'Delivery. Open Toolbox' (MWS410) • 'Delivery. Open Line Toolbox' (MWS411) • 'Delivery. Connect Package' (MWS412) • 'Goods Receiving DO/RO. Open Toolbox' (MWS442). Start 'Sorting order. Open' (CRS022).

cards.question: Page 417 in 'Display Management' (Create Selection Tables) - detail 3: Explain 'The B panel will be displayed' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: The B panel will be displayed. Fill in the Program field and press Enter. Choose option 2=Change in front of your earlier created sorting order and press Enter. Note: You cannot open the F panel with option 1=Create/Select. In the Table field on the (CRS022/F) panel, you press F4 twice to start 'General Selection Table. Open' (CRS023). In the Table field on the (CRS023/B) panel, you give the table a name and choose 1=Create. Press Enter. On the E panel, you fill in the Description and Name fields and then press Enter. M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Display Management

cards.question: Page 418 in 'Display Management' (Create Selection Tables) - detail 1: Explain 'Pressing Enter opens 'Selection Table. Select' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Pressing Enter opens 'Selection Table. Select Fields' (CRS026/B), which is used to create keys or search paths for user-defined tables. Fill in the Field column by pressing F4 twice to start 'Field Group. Display Permitted Fields' (CRS109/B). Select the key fields you want for your user-defined table. Fill in the 'From Value' and 'To Value' fields. Press F3=End to return to (CRS023/B). Select the table you have created and press Enter. Your selected table will be displayed on the (CRS022/F) panel. There is nothing to enter on the T and E panels. Press Enter.

cards.question: Page 418 in 'Display Management' (Create Selection Tables) - detail 2: Explain 'The sorting order you have created' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: The sorting order you have created will be displayed on the (CRS022/B) panel. If you have opened (CRS022) from another program, such as (MWS410), you will automatically return to this program where you can select the sorting order you have created. Otherwise, you need to open the program where you want to use the new sorting order. Note: You can also create selection tables from 'Delivery. Open Toolbox' (MWS410). Use F17= Select to open 'M3 File. Select Fields' (CRS170) In 'Delivery. Open Toolbox' (MWS410) you may choose whether you want to create a selection table from (CRS170) or from (CRS026). If you have set up a selection table in (CRS026), F17 will work as a quick entry to the selection table. Parameters to set The field indicates …FieldProgram ID/ Panel ….the current selected sorting order in use. Each sorting order contains a suggested column lay- out and sorting sequence, plus an optional setup of selections.

cards.question: Page 418 in 'Display Management' (Create Selection Tables) - detail 3: Explain 'Press F4=Browse to browse the present' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Press F4=Browse to browse the present available sorting orders. Sorting order(CRS022/B) …the view that is in use. Views are user-defined and determine the sorting order and the column lay- out. View(CRS022/E) …the program in which the selec- tion table is used. See the field help for valid programs. Program(CRS022/B) ….the fields that are used to cre- ate search paths for user-defined tables. These fields are also used to create the contents of user-de- fined files. Field(CRS026/B) …the from and to values for the specified field. From Value/ To Value(CRS026/B) M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Display Management

cards.question: Page 419 in 'Display Management' (Create Sorting Order Displayed in Browse Windows) - detail 1: Explain 'The field indicates …FieldProgram ID/ Panel' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: The field indicates …FieldProgram ID/ Panel ….the identity of a selection table. Such a table is used to describe which field values in a file should be fulfilled to qualify a record for different purposes. Table(CRS022/F) Create Sorting Order Displayed in Browse Windows This document explains how to create a user defined sorting order, displayed in browse windows (when you press F4=Prompt for searching). In some browse windows, you can select sorting order. In 'Sorting Order in Browse Window. Open' (MNS180) you create user defined sorting orders to be displayed in browse windows.. Note: This function requires a good knowledge of M3 tables and logical views. Outcome User defined sorting order for browse windows is created. The user defined sorting orders are stored in the CMNUQL table.

cards.question: Page 419 in 'Display Management' (Create Sorting Order Displayed in Browse Windows) - detail 2: Explain 'Use this process in browse windows' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Use this process in browse windows where it is possible to select sorting order. Before you start This function requires a good knowledge of M3 tables and logical views. Follow these steps Start 'Sorting Order in Browse Window. Open' (MNS180). Enter the Object field with MITMAS. Define an ID for the sorting order Open the E panel and fill in name. In the File field you fill in the logical view/file you want to be displayed. This is the master file in this sorting order. If you want more than one logical view to be displayed you can fill a secondary file in the 'Join file' field.

cards.question: Page 419 in 'Display Management' (Create Sorting Order Displayed in Browse Windows) - detail 3: Explain 'Open the F panel. The key' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Open the F panel. The key fields in the selected master file are displayed here. The Key type fields indicate how the key fields in the file are to be processed when the file is read. EQ = The field should always be read "equal to" depending on where the inquiry is made. Fill in the Join From/To fields. Note: Fields that will be joined must contain the same number of positions and be of the same type (for example, numeric). M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Display Management

cards.question: Page 420 in 'Display Management' (Create Sorting Order Displayed in Browse Windows) - detail 1: Explain 'Open the G panel. Here is' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Open the G panel. Here is all fields displayed. Fields with 1/xx is in the master file. Fields with 2/xx is in the secondary file. Select which fields to be displayed and in which sequence by entering 10, 20, in the 'Sequence number' field. Open the H panel, continue to the I panel.

cards.question: Page 420 in 'Display Management' (Create Sorting Order Displayed in Browse Windows) - detail 2: Explain 'The field headings are suggested in' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: The field headings are suggested in the 'Column heading and 2' fields. You can change the content in the field headings in Column 1. The 'Field length' field is used to enter the field length for alphanumeric fields. If left blank, the field length in the database is displayed. Press Next and the sorting order is created. Parameters to set This field indicates FieldProgram the table/file for which the browse windows search.

cards.question: Page 420 in 'Display Management' (Create Sorting Order Displayed in Browse Windows) - detail 3: Explain 'Example: the 'Item no' field is' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Example: the 'Item no' field is browsing for MITMAS records. Object(MNS180/B) the user defined identity of the sorting order Sorting order(MNS180/B) the user which is allowed to use this sorting order. User 'blank' means all users are allowed. User(MNS180/B) the logical view (the master view) from where records should be selected File(MNS180/E) the logical view(the secondary view) from where records should be selected. This is used if you want records to be selected from more than one table Join to File(MNS180/E) The key fields included in the view (the master view) that you select- ed Key field(MNS180/F) M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Display Management

cards.question: Page 421 in 'Display Management' (Create Sorting Order Displayed in Browse Windows) - detail 1: Explain 'This field indicates FieldProgram how the' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: This field indicates FieldProgram how the key fields in the file are to be processed when the file is read. The valid alternatives are: EQ = The field should always be read "equal to" depending on where the inquiry is made using F4 = Prompt. SE = The field is opened to allow specification of different values in the window where the result of an F4 = Prompt inquiry is displayed. These values are then read "equal to." Key type(MNS180/F) how the template retrieves infor- mation located in more than one table. These fields are not displayed if you not select 'Join file' on th E panel.

cards.question: Page 421 in 'Display Management' (Create Sorting Order Displayed in Browse Windows) - detail 2: Explain 'The valid alternatives are: = Matching' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: The valid alternatives are: = Matching records. Only selects records that are found in all ta- bles. = Records that match the master file. Selects all records in the master table and the matching records from the secondary table. = Records that do not match the master table.

cards.question: Page 421 in 'Display Management' (Create Sorting Order Displayed in Browse Windows) - detail 3: Explain 'Only selects the records in the' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Only selects the records in the master table that are unique, that is, the ones that do not have matching records in the secondary table. Join type(MNS180/F) how the different fields are to be combined. The field name should begin with 'x' (where x = 1, or 3) to indicate from which file the field is re- trieved. Note: Fields that will be joined must contain the same number of positions and be of the same type (for example, numeric). Join from/ Join to(MNS180/F) M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Display Management

cards.question: Page 422 in 'Display Management' (Configure Browse Sequences) - detail 1: Explain 'This field indicates FieldProgram Where the' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: This field indicates FieldProgram Where the field should be dis- played in the browse window. From left to right. Sequence number(MNS180/G) This field is used to enter the field length for alphanumeric fields. If left blank, the field length in the database is displayed. Field length(MNS180/I) the number of decimal places that the field should contain. The information is only relevant for numerical fields (type S or P). Number of decimal places(MNS180/I) the contents of the heading line. You can change the default con- tent and fill in a user defined con- tent.

cards.question: Page 422 in 'Display Management' (Configure Browse Sequences) - detail 2: Explain 'Column heading and 2(MNS180/I) Configure Browse' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Column heading and 2(MNS180/I) Configure Browse Sequences This instruction explains how to configure specific browse panels. M3 contains three browse options that can be used for fields with user-defined values: • Classic browse: Uses standard functionality with no configuration. This option does not support search. • User-defined sorting orders in classic browse: Configured using 'Sorting Order in Browse Window. Open' (MNS180). This option does not support search. • Configurable browse: Configured using 'Table Browser. Open' (MNS175) based on configurable lists.

cards.question: Page 422 in 'Display Management' (Configure Browse Sequences) - detail 3: Explain 'Supports search. This instruction covers the' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Supports search. This instruction covers the 'Configurable browse' option, which supports all the capabilities of the 'Custom list' in (CMS010) including related tables, virtual fields etc. Outcome A user-defined browse panel is created for a specific scenario. This is displayed when function key F4='Prompt for searching' is used in that scenario, instead of the classic browse panel. Follow these steps Configured browse functions are configured through (MNS175) and uses the list defined in 'Information Browser Category. Open' (CMS010). Note: For best performance, avoid using too many related tables and virtual fields to retrieve information. M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Display Management

cards.question: Page 423 in 'Display Management' (Configure Browse Sequences) - detail 1: Explain 'Launch (MNS175). This setup defines the' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Launch (MNS175). This setup defines the scenarios in which the configured browse panel replaces the classic browse panel. Complete these fields: • Table: Must match the table used by the classic browse. Normally the version of the table is used. Use function key F5 in classic browse to see the table used in the browse. See details in step 3. • Program: The specific program to be used by the configurable browse function. • User: The specific user able to use the configurable browse function. • Field Browse Variant: Enables multiple configurable browse functions to be used. The user can toggle between them by using the Function key F7 in the browse panel. • Description: The description of the browse function. For example, 'Search for items with warehouse filter'.

cards.question: Page 423 in 'Display Management' (Configure Browse Sequences) - detail 2: Explain '• Name: The name that identifies' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • Name: The name that identifies the browse function. • Information category: This must have the 'Used by program' field in (CMS010) set to 'CMS990-Search browser'. The Information category has no relation or validation to the table defined for when it is to be used. For example, a browse function could be set up to browse the item master, but the browse itself could be set up to display information based on the item/warehouse. This means that any information can be used in the browse function, so long as relevant information can be returned to the calling program, for example, the item number. Another example might be using order statistics to browse for sold items. • Sorting order: Defines possible filters to be used as well as the columns to be displayed. • View: Defines the columns to be displayed. • No. filters: Optional. Normally, filters are preset to a value, but they can be defined. For example, a warehouse can be filtered by t

cards.question: Page 423 in 'Display Management' (Configure Browse Sequences) - detail 3: Explain '• Return field: The field to' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • Return field: The field to be returned in the search, for example, 'Item number'. The classic browse function can be used to simplify the setup when deciding which table to use in (MNS175), as follows: a Launch the classic browse in the program and field where the new browse is to be used. b Click Refresh in the classic browse panel. c The table used by the prompt is displayed as either a popup message, or at the bottom of the screen, depending on the UI settings. d Use this table reference number in the (MNS175) record. Using the Configured Browse Panel When a user-defined browse panel is created for a specific scenario, it is automatically displayed unlike the classic browse panel where the function key F4 is used. Using search in the Configured Browse Panel The full search functionality available throughout M3 BE can be used in configured browse. An additional search is also performed, based on the value sent from the program when

cards.question: Page 424 in 'Display Management' (Using Search Functions) - detail 1: Explain 'For example, a browse is configured' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: For example, a browse is configured on the 'Item number' field in the function for the item master table in 'Item. Open' (MMS001). To find items for parmesan cheese, type 'parme' in the 'Item number' field and then click 'Browse'. The panel displayed is automatically filtered as per the query 'parme*'. Note: In (MNS175), you can control if the wildcard character should be automatically appended or not by selecting the 'Remove wildcard' check box. By doing this, only exact matches are returned by the search query. Using Search Functions This document explains how to use the different types of search functions in M3 Business Engine. These types of search are available in M3 BE: • Simple search • Advanced search • Search including reserved words • M3 IES usage through API calls - Infor Enterprise Search (IES); Application Programming Interface (API) • Multi-level key search • Very advanced search queries • Lucene references Simple search T

cards.question: Page 424 in 'Display Management' (Using Search Functions) - detail 2: Explain 'All columns are searched, and the' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: All columns are searched, and the words found may be from multiple columns. • Using the Search box In the Search box, specify the word to be searched for in full and in the correct case. For example, searching for 'cheese' returns all instances of 'cheese' in any column. Using AND with multiple words means that all the words must exist in one or more columns on a line. For example, searching for 'cheese' and 'juice' returns all lines where both words exist in any column on a line. Using OR with multiple words means that one of the words must exist in one of the columns. For example, searching for 'cheese OR juice' returns all lines where either word exists in one of the columns. Use ( ) to combine blocks of AND and OR.

cards.question: Page 424 in 'Display Management' (Using Search Functions) - detail 3: Explain 'For example, 'cheese OR (juice 011215)'' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: For example, 'cheese OR (juice 011215)' will return all lines where either 'cheese' exists or 'juice 011215' exists. You can use these wildcards in the Search box: • '*' - replaces more than one character. For example, 'chee*' returns all instances containing 'chee' such as 'cheese', 'cheeses', 'large cheeses', etc. • '?' - replaces a single character in a specific position. For example, 'jui?e' returns instances of 'juice'. • Columns To limit the search to a single column, right-click the column header and select 'Add to search'. Specify the search word. M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Display Management

cards.question: Page 425 in 'Display Management' (Using Search Functions) - detail 1: Explain '• Field names You can use' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • Field names You can use field names in the search query to search in fields that are not displayed as a column. For example, 'chee* STAT:20' returns instances containing 'chee' and which also have the status of '20', even though the status is not displayed in a column. • Dates You can search for specific dates, using the same date format as defined for the user in the user master. For example, 'LMDT: 112515' returns all lines with '112515' in the date column in that format. • Decimal points The decimal point format used is controlled by the setting on the user master. For example, '.', ',' etc. Advanced search • Ranges You can search for ranges of data by using brackets as follows: • [x TO y] means the values are included. For example, '[ TO 480]' returns all instances in the selected column containing values from to 480. • {x TO y} means the values are excluded.

cards.question: Page 425 in 'Display Management' (Using Search Functions) - detail 2: Explain 'For example, '{ TO 480}' returns' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: For example, '{ TO 480}' returns all instances in the selected column outside the range to 480. Ranges can use wildcards and combinations of '[' and '}' to find all transactions with a negative value. • Dates These date macros are provided: • YEAR(x) • MONTH(x) • WEEK(x) • DATE(x) 'x' is a relative number with '0' being the current year, month, week, and date. For example, 'INDT:YEAR(-1)'. The DATE macro can be combined with ranges to create your own range. For example, 'LMDT:[DATE(-14) TO DATE(0)] returns all transactions within the last two weeks. When searching with NOT, this must be preceded by another query. For example, 'No: NOT INDT:YEAR(0)'. Date macros are very useful with saved searches as they give a quick link to, for example, last month's orders.

cards.question: Page 425 in 'Display Management' (Using Search Functions) - detail 3: Explain 'They can be saved to Favorites' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: They can be saved to Favorites, Canvas, as a menu option, or in the toolbox area, so they can be easily accessed. • Wildcards '?' - replaces a single character in a specific position and can be used together with other wildcards. For example, 'SUNO:?*'. For numeric fields you can use the NOT statement. For example nb mkll?>e, 'NOT STQT:0'. '~' – can be used in alphabetic characters, not numeric characters and is a fuzzy search. This means that the characters do not need to exactly match those in the query. • Sorting search results M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Display Management

cards.question: Page 426 in 'Display Management' (Using Search Functions) - detail 1: Explain 'Sorting search results allows you to' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Sorting search results allows you to free sort any field in the main table of the function, apart from fields containing multiple words such as a description. A search finding results in a related table, set up in 'Related Search. Open' (CMS022) are not sorted. A search which finds results in both related tables and the main table presents hits for the main table first in the selected sorting method, followed by hits from the related tables which are not sorted. This sort is done by using the keyword 'SortBy:x,a,!y' where '!' performs a descending order. You can save sorting in the toolbox area which enables you to re-sort the list without having to use a separate index. For example, you could re-sort results for the current company number using 'CONO:xxx', where 'xxx' is replaced by the currently used M3 BE company number. • Searching multiple words Searching multiple words across several columns can easily return false results as it r

cards.question: Page 426 in 'Display Management' (Using Search Functions) - detail 2: Explain 'By using the keyword 'SearchFields:x;y;z', you' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: By using the keyword 'SearchFields:x;y;z', you can search in multiple columns but only in the specified fields. For example, 'SearchFields:ITDS,FUDS chees* 10'. • Key search Searching with the help of key search simplifies complex queries so they are easier to use. It also allows searching in other tables without the need for matching keys, as required by the related search setup in (CMS022). For example, you can define a query which, when specified, the preset part in the key search is automatically appended. Available key searches are displayed by using Ctrl+F. Note: A key search is performed on both the main table and related tables (defined in (CMS022) or related tables through 'Related Tables. Connect' (CMS011)). Searching with reserved words M3 IES (Infor Enterprise Search) Reserved Words are used in stored and published search queries to automatically retrieve information from the context they belong to.

cards.question: Page 426 in 'Display Management' (Using Search Functions) - detail 3: Explain 'By using reserved words, you can' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: By using reserved words, you can build and publish general widgets, favorite pages in M3 H5, and home pages and widgets in Infor OS. Reserved words can also replace information in queries based on a selected line in a list, or from a panel header. Saved search can also be used to add links to other programs where the search query is passed to the next program. This table shows M3 IES (Infor Enterprise Search) reserved words: EffectReserved word Sorts search result in x, y, z hierarchy. The '!' prior to a field name performs a descending sort order. SortBy:x,y,!z Limits search to listed fields x, y etc. Increases performance and can eliminate false hits. SearchFields:x;y Date range of year relative to current year x=- means last year.Year(x) M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Display Management

cards.question: Page 427 in 'Display Management' (Using Search Functions) - detail 1: Explain 'EffectReserved word Date range of month' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: EffectReserved word Date range of month relative to current month x=- means last month.Month(x) Date range of week relative to current week x=- means last week.Week(x) Date range of date relative to current date x=- means previous day.Date(x) List & list header based replacements to query string.<xxxx> Logged on user ID.<USID> Logged on company number.<CONO> Logged on Division; same as <CurrentDivision>.<DIVI> Default facility from 'User. Open' (MNS150).<FACI> Customer number from (MNS150).<CUNO> Language code from (MNS150).<LNCD> Currently used language in M3 BE (in case of switched language during session).<CurrentLanguage> Time zone from (MNS150).<TIZO> Warehouse from (MNS150).<WHLO> Department from (MNS150).<DEPT> Menu version from (MNS150).<MNVR> Menu name from (MNS150).<DFMN> This table shows M3 IES (Infor Enterprise Search) for use with key search only: EffectReserved word Specified query in the search string.<QRY> Used to change

cards.question: Page 427 in 'Display Management' (Using Search Functions) - detail 2: Explain 'NOOPT Using stop words M3 IES' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: NOOPT Using stop words M3 IES Stop Words are words that are not indexed and are automatically removed from the search query. For example, 'and', 'or, and 'etc'. IES, by default, has a set of English-based stop words which also include 'a', 'by', 'is', and 'as'. There may be cases where these words have an alternate meaning which does need to be included in a search.

cards.question: Page 427 in 'Display Management' (Using Search Functions) - detail 3: Explain 'For example, 'Factor a' or the' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: For example, 'Factor a' or the Swedish word 'is' which means 'ice' in English. The workaround for this is to add a new stop word in the IED administration pages for those words. This would be called something unique that would not be searched for otherwise, such as 'lkdshja7789'. M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Display Management

cards.question: Page 428 in 'Display Management' (Using Search Functions) - detail 1: Explain 'Note: The affected tables such as' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Note: The affected tables such as MITMAS, MITLAD, OCUSMA, CIDMAS etc., must be re-indexed after adding new stop words in the IED administration pages. Search query and key search rules These rules are used to set '*' and '~': • <QRY>* - Automatically appends a wild card to each word in the specified query. For example, the query 'joh smit' is set to 'joh* smit*' before the search is performed. • <QRY>~' - Automatically appends a tilde, or fuzzy search, to each word in the specified query. For example, the query 'joh smit' is set to 'joh~ smit~' before the search is performed. Fuzzy searches should not be used with numeric values due to the nature of this type of search. Exceptions: • Query inside "" - When specifying a query enclosed by double quotation marks "", wildcards are not added to the search as the search engine treats all characters contained within "" as normal characters. That is, "joh*" only returns results for 'joh*' inclu

cards.question: Page 428 in 'Display Management' (Using Search Functions) - detail 2: Explain 'This is to improve system performance' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: This is to improve system performance. M3 Infor Enterprise Search (IES) usage through Application Programming Interface (API) calls M3 API fully supports the use of IES. Some standard API transactions are search enabled, such as 'Trans SearchItem' MMS200MI, where the normal filter input is replaced by a search query. The search through API supports all normal search queries, except the UI related ones such as Date macros and field value replacements, such as <WHLO>. MDBREADMI and CMS100MI are fully search-enabled. Note: There is no fixed upper limit of records that can be returned by a search in an API, but there is a practical limit caused by a timeout. Therefore, the number of records returned depends on the environment, but a reasonable maximum is around 3.000-6. records. For more details, see the API Repository in 'MI Repository. Open' (MRS001), 'MI Transaction. Open' (MRS002), and 'MI Transaction Layout.

cards.question: Page 428 in 'Display Management' (Using Search Functions) - detail 3: Explain 'Open' (MRS003). Facet searches A feature' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Open' (MRS003). Facet searches A feature of the IES through API calls is facet search. These are basically a count of the number of records that match a certain value in a specific field, or aggregating the number of records into various groups. M3 facets are retrieved through (FACETIM). The input to the API is the table name for which the facets are retrieved, and secondly by the search query for filtering the facets and their count. For example, results of a query might be grouped as records with a purchase price of '0' and record with a sales price of '20'. The advantage of using facets is high performance regardless of the number of records, and the filter of the facets and their count is based on a search query. This provides endless possible drill arounds in the data. Facets can also be used as on-line statistics and they have been added to many M3 tables, including transactional and statistical tables. For example, facets are use

cards.question: Page 429 in 'Display Management' (Using Search Functions) - detail 1: Explain 'Multi-level key search Multi-level key search' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Multi-level key search Multi-level key search is used to search across multiple tables in parallel. Note: You must always start a multi-level search with the search returning the least number of hits which is also the one where you use the specified query, for example, the MITMAS level. Adding several key searches makes it possible to search descriptions for different languages as well as aliases, using the same query. Note that you connect key search to a higher level in the same way as if there was only a single key search.

cards.question: Page 429 in 'Display Management' (Using Search Functions) - detail 2: Explain 'Further advanced search queries This section' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Further advanced search queries This section is targeted at advanced IES users wanting to run IES search queries through code. These are not created manually. Key search without Ctrl+F UI/(CMS030) Example of syntax used when defined in 'Key Search. Open' (CMS030): related:[ITEM/WHS("WHLO:100"; ITEM GRP1("ITGR:1533"; ITEM_BROWS("chair")))] • Key search ID that exists in (CMS030) – 'ITEM/WHS', 'ITEM GRP1, and 'ITEM_BROWS' • Query per key search – 'WHLO:100', 'ITGR:1533', and 'chair' Note: It is important to ensure the correct number of ')' are used.

cards.question: Page 429 in 'Display Management' (Using Search Functions) - detail 3: Explain 'Key search without UI/(CMS030) Example of' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Key search without UI/(CMS030) Example of syntax used when not defined in (CMS030): related: [MITMAS(ITNO;"ITGR:fop*"; ITEM_BROWS("chair"))] • Tablename in M3 - 'MITMAS' • Output fields from key search (use ',' if searching multiple fields) – 'ITNO' • Query per key search – 'ITGR:fop' and 'chair' • Key search ID that exists in (CMS030) – 'ITEM_BROWS' Example of syntax used when not defined in (CMS030) and alias not in 'Field Alias. Open' (CMS031): related:[ITEM_RESP("magnus";MITBAL(RESP=USID,CHID;"RESP:itama0";ITEM/WHS("WHLO:100";ITEM GRP2("1533";ITEM_BROWS("chair")))))] • Tablename in M3 – 'MITBAL' • Output fields from key search (use ',' if searching multiple fields) – 'USID','CHID' • Key search ID that exists in (CMS030) – 'ITEM_RESP', 'ITEM/WHS' and 'ITEM_BROWS' • Field alias (normally defined in (CMS031)) – 'RESP' Example using key search • related:[ORDEREDGTO_1(MITMAS(ITNO;"ITNO:cherry OR ITDS:cherry~ OR FUDS:cherry~ OR ITDS:cherr

cards.question: Page 430 in 'Display Management' (Connect Information Views from Different Programs) - detail 1: Explain '• related:[MITMAS(ITNO;"ITGR:fop*"; ITEM_BROWS("chair"))] NOT related:[ITEM/WHS("WHLO:100] Separate' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • related:[MITMAS(ITNO;"ITGR:fop*"; ITEM_BROWS("chair"))] NOT related:[ITEM/WHS("WHLO:100] Separate key search. You can use AND (default if nothing specified), OR and NOT. In this example, hits that exist in the first part are listed as long as they do not hit in the second key search. • MAXJOIN: related:[MITMAS(ITNO;"ITGR:fop*"; ITEM_BROWS("chair"))] Note that MAXJOIN increases the number of hits internally in the key search. This overrides the setting in the IES local management pages. Lucene references IES uses the Lucene search engine as a base. Lucene search syntax is documented in https://lucene.apache.org/core/2_9_4/queryparsersyntax.html. Connect Information Views from Different Programs This document explains how you create a connection between information views from different programs.

cards.question: Page 430 in 'Display Management' (Connect Information Views from Different Programs) - detail 2: Explain 'When working in a certain program' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: When working in a certain program that starts another program, you will be able to go directly to a specific information view in the started program. Outcome A connection between information views in different programs are created. Sorting orders are stored in the (CSYSVIU) table and views in (CSYSPV). The connections created in (CRS014) are stored in the (CRINPA) table. You choose the information views that will be displayed when you start a specified program from another (also specified) program. In this way you will get exactly the information you need. Before you start Make sure that it is possible to connect information views between the programs. This information will be displayed if you press F4 =Prompt, on the (CRS014/B) panel, in the field 'To Program'.

cards.question: Page 430 in 'Display Management' (Connect Information Views from Different Programs) - detail 3: Explain 'Follow these steps Connect Information Views' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Follow these steps Connect Information Views from Different Programs Start 'Program Start Values. Open' (CRS014). The B panel is the opening panel. Depending on what sorting order you choose on the B panel, the order of the fields displayed will vary. The valid alternatives for the sorting order are: 1=To program, From program, Responsible 2= From program, To program, Responsible 3= Responsible, To program, From program. Choose option 1=Create/Select. M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Display Management

cards.question: Page 431 in 'Display Management' (Connect Information Views from Different Programs) - detail 1: Explain 'On the (CRS014/B) panel in the' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: On the (CRS014/B) panel in the 'To program' field, you fill in the program that you will reach via the created connection. In the 'From program' field, you fill in the program where you start. Enter your user name in the Responsible field. Press Enter: The E panel is displayed. In the 'Sorting order' field, you choose an sorting order for the started program. Press F4 once to choose an already existing sorting order. If you want to create a new sorting order, press F4 once more and you will get to 'Sorting order. Open' (CRS022). See Manage Sorting Orders on page 406. If you do not set a value, M3 will retrieve the same sorting order that was used the last time the To program was started.

cards.question: Page 431 in 'Display Management' (Connect Information Views from Different Programs) - detail 2: Explain 'On the (CRS014/E) panel, fill in' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: On the (CRS014/E) panel, fill in the 'View' field for the started program. Press F4 once if you want to select an already existing view. If you press F4 once more, you will get to 'View. Open' (CRS020) where you can create a new view. See Manage Views on page 411. For programs with user-defined sorting orders, the view used in the started program will be the one connected to the sorting order. In the 'Panel sequence' field on the E panel, you specify which panels you want to be displayed, as well as the sequence in which they should appear. For programs with user-defined sorting orders, the panel sequence used in the started program will be the one connected to the sorting order specified in (CRS014). In the 'Opening panel' field, specify the first panel to be displayed. This panel is valid until changed by the user.

cards.question: Page 431 in 'Display Management' (Connect Information Views from Different Programs) - detail 3: Explain 'Note that the valid opening panels' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Note that the valid opening panels can differ between programs. The 'List records' field is very important when you have the To program set as (MMS200), (MWS410), etc. Select *NODSP to gain performance. Press Next to display the B panel. Press F14 to activate the connection. Parameters to set The field indicates …FieldProgram ID/ Panel … for which started program the setup is valid. To program(CRS014/B) … for which starting program the setup is valid. If this information is blank, the setup is valid when the To program is started from the menu. From program(CRS014/B) M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Display Management

cards.question: Page 432 in 'Display Management' (Connect Information Views from Different Programs) - detail 1: Explain 'The field indicates …FieldProgram ID/ Panel' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: The field indicates …FieldProgram ID/ Panel … the user for whom the setup is valid. If this information is left blank, the setup will be valid for all users. Note: If 'User defined field' is set to *NODSP=Not list initial records, then Responsible should be left blank=valid for all users. Responsible(CRS014/B) … which sorting order should be used for the started program.

cards.question: Page 432 in 'Display Management' (Connect Information Views from Different Programs) - detail 2: Explain 'If this value is zero, M3' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: If this value is zero, M3 will retrieve the same sorting order that was used the last time the To program was started. Sorting order(CRS014/E) … the view in use. Views are user defined and determine what fields are to be displayed, as well as how the data is to be calculated. View(CRS014/E) …which panels should be dis- played, as well as the sequence in which they appear.

cards.question: Page 432 in 'Display Management' (Connect Information Views from Different Programs) - detail 3: Explain 'Panel sequence(CRS014/E) … the panel in' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Panel sequence(CRS014/E) … the panel in the started pro- gram that will be the opening panel. Opening panel(CRS014/E) M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Display Management

cards.question: Page 433 in 'Display Management' (Information Browser Categories) - detail 1: Explain 'The field indicates …FieldProgram ID/ Panel' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: The field indicates …FieldProgram ID/ Panel … whether the displayed program should prevent an automatic list- ing of qualified records on the B panel when called from another program, from the menu, or when changing the sorting order. The valid alternatives are: blank = All records listed (stan- dard) *NODSP = Records not listed ini- tially (to gain performance). When *NODSP is activated, the user must refresh the B panel be- fore any records are displayed. The purpose of this is to enable the user to fill in the actual filter values before reading the database, and thereby avoid un- necessary reads in order to gain performance. List records(CRS014/E) Information Browser Categories Outcome Information browser categories are defined in 'Information Browser Category. Open' (CMS010).

cards.question: Page 433 in 'Display Management' (Information Browser Categories) - detail 2: Explain 'The actual data to display is' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: The actual data to display is retrieved from the master table selected for the information browser category in this program. All sorting is based on that master table. An Xtend M3 table is also used as a master table. Follow these steps To use an information browser category, you must activate (CMS100) first in (CMS010) by selecting alternative 'Activate' from the 'Related' field or press Ctrl+7. Select from these alternatives which is used for the information browser categories: • Deactivate an information browser category: Use alternative 'Deactivate' from the Related field or press Ctrl+8. When used, a dialog box appears to confirm the transaction: • If Yes is selected, Sorting order, View, and Field group will be lost, and the information browser category status is set to 10.

cards.question: Page 433 in 'Display Management' (Information Browser Categories) - detail 3: Explain '• If Cancel is selected, nothing' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • If Cancel is selected, nothing changes, and the transaction is canceled. • Manage related tables. Use alternative 'Related tables' from the 'Related' field or press Ctrl+11. See Manage Related Tables on page 434. M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Display Management

cards.question: Page 434 in 'Display Management' (Manage Related Tables) - detail 1: Explain '• Manage virtual fields. Use alternative' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • Manage virtual fields. Use alternative 'Virtual fields' from the 'Related' field or press Ctrl+12. See Manage Virtual Fields on page 438. • Manage related options and set up a related option for each information category: Use alternative 'Related options' from the 'Related' field or press Ctrl+13. See specific chapter on related options. • Manage available fields: Use alternative 'Field group' from the 'Related' field or press Ctrl+21. See Field Group on page 406. • Manage views: Use alternative 'View' from the 'Related' field or press Ctrl+22. See Manage Views on page 411. • Manage sorting orders: Use alternative 'Sorting Order' from the 'Related' field or press Ctrl+23. See Manage Sorting Orders on page 406.

cards.question: Page 434 in 'Display Management' (Manage Related Tables) - detail 2: Explain '• Design and simulate the list' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: • Design and simulate the list: Use alternative 'Simulate list' from the 'Related' field or press Ctrl+24. • When selecting 'Simulate list', the program for customer list design is opened. This offers a preview of the selected list and design throughout (CMS100). • Export configuration: Use alternative 'Export Configuration' from the 'Related' field or press Ctrl+26. By exporting the configuration, you can save or transfer the settings of a printer file or list program. Use alternative 'Export Configuration' or press Ctrl+ and specify the file name and version number of the configuration. • Locate the file by opening 'Configuration Data Jobs. Open' (MNS200): Use sorting order 4, then specify the file name and start date. Use alternative 'Display XML file' from the 'Related' or press Ctrl+ to download the XML file as a compressed zip file. Note: Information browser categories starting with M3 in (CMS010) are standard configurations inclu

cards.question: Page 434 in 'Display Management' (Manage Related Tables) - detail 3: Explain 'Instead, you can make a copy' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
cards.goodAnswer: Instead, you can make a copy of it to make changes. Manage Related Tables In this document the methods are described for displaying fields from other BE tables or XtendM3 tables related to the master table by defining related tables in the program 'Related Tables. Connect' (CMS011). Follow these steps Open (CMS011) by using alternative 11='Related tables' in these programs: • 'List and Printer Programs. Configure' (CMS005) • 'XML Structure Section. Open Element' (CMS009) • 'Information Browser Category. Open' (CMS010) In (CMS011), you can define up to related tables. To create a related table on the (CMS011/B) panel, specify the table. Use alternative 1='Create'. M3 Application Foundation User Guide | Display Management

cards.question: Page 435 in 'Display Management' (Manage Related Tables) - detail 1: Explain 'Parameters to set The field indicates' and list the concrete admin rules, configuration points, or process steps from this page.
